built with pdoc

heaven_base

HEAVEN Base - Hierarchical, Embodied, Autonomously Validating Evolution Network Complete core framework for building autonomous AI agents.

  1"""
  2HEAVEN Base - Hierarchical, Embodied, Autonomously Validating Evolution Network
  3Complete core framework for building autonomous AI agents.
  4"""
  5
  6# Redirect print() to stderr to prevent MCP protocol corruption
  7# Set HEAVEN_ALLOW_STDOUT=1 to disable this (e.g. for docker cross-machine scenarios)
  8import sys
  9import os
 10import builtins
 11
 12if os.environ.get("HEAVEN_ALLOW_STDOUT") != "1":
 13    _original_print = builtins.print
 14    def _stderr_print(*args, **kwargs):
 15        kwargs.setdefault('file', sys.stderr)
 16        _original_print(*args, **kwargs)
 17    builtins.print = _stderr_print
 18
 19__version__ = "0.1.21"

__version__ = '0.1.21'
class BaseHeavenAgent(abc.ABC): 
 716class BaseHeavenAgent(ABC):
 717    """Base class for GOD Framework agents with task management."""
 718    
 719    def __init__(self, config: HeavenAgentConfig, unified_chat: UnifiedChat, max_tool_calls: int = 10, orchestrator: bool = False, history: Optional[History] = None, history_id = None, system_prompt_suffix: Optional[str] = None, adk: Optional[bool] = False, duo_enabled: Optional[bool] = False, run_on_langchain: Optional[bool] = False, use_uni_api: Optional[bool] = False):
 720        # Configure root logger to output to stdout
 721        logging.basicConfig(
 722            stream=sys.stdout,
 723            level=logging.DEBUG,  # Adjust the level as needed
 724            format='%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(name)s: %(message)s'
 725            )
 726        logging.getLogger('watchdog').setLevel(logging.WARNING)
 727        
 728        if config is None:
 729            raise ValueError("config cannot be None")
 730        if unified_chat is None:
 731            raise ValueError("unified_chat cannot be None")
 732        self.use_uni_api = use_uni_api
 733        self.unified_chat = unified_chat
 734        self.known_config_paths = getattr(config, "known_config_paths", None)
 735        if self.known_config_paths is None:
 736            self.known_config_paths = []
 737        self.training_convo = [
 738            SystemMessage(content=""),
 739            HumanMessage(content="Hey I need to teach you how to use the tools correctly before we get started. Once you learn how to use this tool I'm going to show you, you'll know how to handle all of our tools. Essentially, it comes down to acknowledging the errors correctly. For example, can you use NetworkEditTool to write a file in tmp while having no file_content parameter, read the error that is sent back from the tool, and then adjust? file_content is an optional parameter in the NetworkEditTool, but it is actually required for certain operations, like the create command."),
 740            AIMessage(content=[{"text": "Following the instructions to first try without file_text and then fix it, I'll write utility code for an AST analyzer as an example.\n\nFirst attempt (should fail):", "type": "text"}]),
 741            AIMessage(content=[{"type": "tool_use", "id": "toolu_01BPHfZNPMY1tbnsRtRYmG5i", "name": "NetworkEditTool", "input": {"command": "create", "target_container": "creation_of_god", "path": "/tmp/ast_analyzer_utils.py"}}]),
 742            ToolMessage(content="ERROR: Parameter `file_text` is required for command: create", tool_call_id="toolu_01BPHfZNPMY1tbnsRtRYmG5i"),
 743            AIMessage(content=[{"text": "As expected, we need to include file_text. Here's the proper attempt with the utility code:", "type": "text"}]),
 744            AIMessage(content=[{"type": "tool_use", "id": "toolu_01LS6GZDEpEqDVmU8qpvRa65", "name": "NetworkEditTool", "input": {"command": "create", "target_container": "creation_of_god", "path": "/tmp/ast_analyzer_utils.py", "file_text": "\"\"\"AST analyzer utilities for extracting concise code context and dependencies.\"\"\"\n\nimport ast\nfrom typing import Dict, List, Set, Union, Optional\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n\n@dataclass\nclass CodeContext:\n    \"\"\"Data class to hold analyzed code context.\"\"\"\n    imports: List[str]\n    dependencies: Dict[str, Set[str]]\n    call_graph: Dict[str, List[str]]\n\ndef parse_code_to_ast(code: str) -> ast.AST:\n    \"\"\"Parse code string into AST.\"\"\"\n    return ast.parse(code)\n\ndef extract_imports(tree: ast.AST) -> List[str]:\n    \"\"\"Extract all import statements from AST.\"\"\"\n    imports = []\n    for node in ast.walk(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, ast.Import):\n            for name in node.names:\n                imports.append(name.name)\n        elif isinstance(node, ast.ImportFrom):\n            module = node.module or ''\n            for name in node.names:\n                if name.name == '*':\n                    imports.append(f\"from {module} import *\")\n                else:\n                    imports.append(f\"from {module} import {name.name}\")\n    return sorted(imports)\n\ndef analyze_dependencies(tree: ast.AST) -> Dict[str, Set[str]]:\n    \"\"\"Analyze function/class dependencies.\"\"\"\n    dependencies = {}\n    current_func = None\n    \n    class DependencyVisitor(ast.NodeVisitor):\n        def visit_FunctionDef(self, node):\n            nonlocal current_func\n            old_func = current_func\n            current_func = node.name\n            if current_func not in dependencies:\n                dependencies[current_func] = set()\n            self.generic_visit(node)\n            current_func = old_func\n            \n        def visit_Call(self, node):\n            if current_func and isinstance(node.func, ast.Name):\n                dependencies[current_func].add(node.func.id)\n            self.generic_visit(node)\n    \n    DependencyVisitor().visit(tree)\n    return dependencies\n\ndef generate_call_graph(dependencies: Dict[str, Set[str]]) -> Dict[str, List[str]]:\n    \"\"\"Generate simplified call graph from dependencies.\"\"\"\n    return {\n        func: sorted(list(calls))\n        for func, calls in dependencies.items()\n    }\n\ndef analyze_code_context(code: str) -> CodeContext:\n    \"\"\"Main function to analyze code and return context.\"\"\"\n    tree = parse_code_to_ast(code)\n    imports = extract_imports(tree)\n    dependencies = analyze_dependencies(tree)\n    call_graph = generate_call_graph(dependencies)\n    \n    return CodeContext(\n        imports=imports,\n        dependencies=dependencies,\n        call_graph=call_graph\n    )"}}]),
 745            ToolMessage(content="File created successfully at: /tmp/ast_analyzer_utils.py", tool_call_id="toolu_01LS6GZDEpEqDVmU8qpvRa65"),
 746            AIMessage(content="The utility code has been written with the following key components..."),
 747            HumanMessage(content="Thanks! Now you know how to use tools correctly in our system. BTW, I deleted that file for you because this was just a learning experience. I also removed the NetworkEditTool for now. I'll equip it on you again later if you need it for the real goal we are working on. Now we're going to get on to the real goal..."),
 748            AIMessage(content="Perfect! I'm ready. Let me know how what else I can do for you.")
 749        ]
 750        self.known_config_paths = [str(path) for path in self.known_config_paths]
 751        self.hooks = config.hook_registry
 752        # Persona resolution: if persona set, load from SkillManager and extract components
 753        self.carton_identity = config.carton_identity
 754        # Set agent context for SkillTool so it uses agent-scoped SkillManager
 755        from .tool_utils.skill_utils import set_agent_context
 756        set_agent_context(config.name)
 757        if config.persona:
 758            try:
 759                from skill_manager.core import SkillManager
 760                sm = SkillManager(agent_id=config.name)
 761                persona_obj = sm.get_persona(config.persona)
 762                if persona_obj:
 763                    # Frame → prepend to system prompt
 764                    if persona_obj.frame and persona_obj.frame.strip():
 765                        config.system_prompt = persona_obj.frame + "\n\n" + config.system_prompt
 766                    # Skillset → use persona's if not explicitly set on config
 767                    if not config.skillset and persona_obj.skillset:
 768                        config.skillset = persona_obj.skillset
 769                    # MCP set → use persona's if not explicitly set on config
 770                    if not config.mcp_set and persona_obj.mcp_set:
 771                        config.mcp_set = persona_obj.mcp_set
 772                    # CartON identity → use persona's if not explicitly set
 773                    if not self.carton_identity and persona_obj.carton_identity:
 774                        self.carton_identity = persona_obj.carton_identity
 775            except Exception:
 776                pass  # Persona resolution is best-effort, never block agent startup
 777        # MCP set resolution: resolve set name → two paths per MCP:
 778        #   1. MCP IS in strata → load DIRECTLY on agent from strata config
 779        #   2. All MCPs → equip mcp-skill-* if exists (for context/instructions)
 780        # Pattern: hierarchical_summarize/flow.py::_get_summarizer_mcp_servers()
 781        self._mcp_skill_names = []
 782        if config.mcp_set:
 783            try:
 784                from strata.config import MCPServerList
 785                server_list = MCPServerList()
 786                set_server_names = server_list.get_set(config.mcp_set)
 787                if set_server_names:
 788                    from skill_manager.core import SkillManager
 789                    sm = SkillManager(agent_id=config.name)
 790                    for srv_name in set_server_names:
 791                        # Equip mcp-skill-* if it exists (context about the MCP)
 792                        skill_name = f"mcp-skill-{srv_name.lower()}"
 793                        skill = sm.get_skill(skill_name)
 794                        if skill:
 795                            self._mcp_skill_names.append(skill_name)
 796                            sm.equip(skill_name)
 797                        # If MCP IS in strata, load it directly on the agent
 798                        srv_config = server_list.get_server(srv_name)
 799                        if srv_config and srv_config.enabled:
 800                            if config.mcp_servers is None:
 801                                config.mcp_servers = {}
 802                            config.mcp_servers[srv_name] = {
 803                                "command": srv_config.command,
 804                                "args": srv_config.args,
 805                                "env": srv_config.env,
 806                                "transport": "stdio",
 807                            }
 808            except Exception as e:
 809                import logging as _log
 810                _log.getLogger(__name__).warning("MCP set resolution failed for %s: %s", config.mcp_set, e)
 811        # Auto-register default skill hooks when skillset is configured
 812        if config.skillset:
 813            from .hooks.default_hooks import register_skill_hooks
 814            register_skill_hooks(self.hooks, agent_name=config.name or "unnamed", skillset_name=config.skillset)
 815        self.max_tool_calls = max_tool_calls
 816        self.config = config
 817        self.name = config.name if config.name is not None else "unnamed_agent"
 818        # Convert and store LangChain config
 819        self.config_dict = config.to_langchain_config()
 820        # initialize status
 821        self.status = AgentStatus()
 822        self.continuation_prompt = ""
 823        self.continuation_iterations: int = 0
 824        # Store config tool classes
 825        self.config_tools = config.tools
 826        self.adk = adk
 827        # if not run_on_langchain:
 828        #     self.adk = True
 829        if run_on_langchain:
 830            self.adk = False
 831        if use_uni_api:
 832            self.adk = False
 833        self.additional_kws = config.additional_kws
 834        self.additional_kw_instructions = config.additional_kw_instructions
 835        # Instantiate the tools
 836        self.resolved_tools = self.resolve_tools()
 837        self.tools = []
 838        self.mcp_tool_strs = []  # Store MCP strings separately
 839        for tool in self.resolved_tools:
 840            if isinstance(tool, str) and tool.startswith("mcp__"):
 841                # MCP tool string reference - store separately for later resolution
 842                self.mcp_tool_strs.append(tool)
 843            elif isinstance(tool, (StructuredTool, BaseTool)):
 844                # Already a LangChain tool instance (e.g., from MCP)
 845                # Just add it directly - same as BaseHeavenTool.create() output
 846                self.tools.append(tool)
 847            elif hasattr(tool, 'create'):
 848                # BaseHeavenTool subclass - use its create method
 849                self.tools.append(tool.create(adk))
 850            else:
 851                print(f"Unknown tool type: {tool}, skipping")  
 852        # Filter and prepare provider-specific parameters
 853
 854        model_params = {
 855            'provider': config.provider,
 856            'model': config.model,
 857            'temperature': config.temperature,
 858            'max_tokens': config.max_tokens,
 859            'thinking_budget': config.thinking_budget
 860        }
 861        if config.extra_model_kwargs:
 862            model_params.update(config.extra_model_kwargs)
 863        # Create chat model internally using UnifiedChat
 864        self.chat_model = unified_chat.create(**model_params)
 865        self.resolve_duo(config)
 866        self.duo_params = config.duo_system_config.get_duo_params() if config.duo_system_config is not None else None
 867        self.duo_enabled = duo_enabled
 868        self.duo_chat = unified_chat.create(**self.duo_params)
 869        self.duo_system_prompt = config.duo_system_config.system_prompt if config.duo_system_config is not None else ""
 870        # Bind tools if available
 871        if self.tools and not self.adk:
 872        
 873            # For ADK, tools are already in the right format
 874            
 875            # Original LangChain binding logic
 876            if config.provider in [ProviderEnum.OPENAI, ProviderEnum.DEEPSEEK]:
 877                raw_provider_schemas = [tool.get_openai_function() for tool in self.tools]
 878                
 879                self.chat_model = self.chat_model.bind_tools(raw_provider_schemas)
 880              
 881            elif config.provider in [ProviderEnum.GOOGLE]:
 882                raw_provider_schemas = [tool.get_openai_function() for tool in self.tools]
 883                flattened_schemas = [generate_dereferenced_schema(schema) for schema in raw_provider_schemas]
 884                fixed_schemas = [fix_empty_object_properties(schema) for schema in flattened_schemas]
 885                
 886                self.chat_model = self.chat_model.bind_tools(fixed_schemas)
 887              
 888            else:
 889              
 890                self.chat_model = self.chat_model.bind_tools(self.to_base_tools())
 891                
 892                    
 893            # if config.provider in [ProviderEnum.OPENAI, ProviderEnum.DEEPSEEK]:
 894            #     # For these providers, use the schema generated by to_openai_function
 895                
 896            #     # Create the list of schema dictionaries using the classmethod
 897            #     # This list holds dictionaries like {'type': 'function', 'function': {...}}
 898            #     raw_provider_schemas = [
 899            #         # tool.__class__.to_openai_function()
 900            #         tool.get_openai_function()
 901            #         for tool in self.tools
 902            #     ]
 903    
 904            #     # Bind using bind_tools, passing the list of schema dictionaries
 905            #     # as per the apparent design of convert_to_openai_function's output usage
 906            #     print(f"Attempting bind_tools with {len(raw_provider_schemas)} generated schema dictionaries...")
 907            #     self.chat_model = self.chat_model.bind_tools(raw_provider_schemas)
 908            #     print(f"Binding via bind_tools with generated schemas successful.")
 909            # elif config.provider in [ProviderEnum.GOOGLE]:
 910            #     raw_provider_schemas = [tool.get_openai_function() for tool in self.tools]
 911            #     flattened_schemas = [generate_dereferenced_schema(schema) for schema in raw_provider_schemas]
 912            #     # Apply the fixer to remove empty object properties.
 913            #     fixed_schemas = [fix_empty_object_properties(schema) for schema in flattened_schemas]
 914            #     print(f"Attempting bind_tools with {len(fixed_schemas)} generated flattened schema dictionaries...")
 915            #     self.chat_model = self.chat_model.bind_tools(fixed_schemas)
 916            #     print("Binding via bind_tools with generated flattened schemas successful.")
 917            # else:
 918            #     self.chat_model = self.chat_model.bind_tools(self.to_base_tools())
 919                        
 920        # Agentic state
 921        
 922        self.goal: Optional[str] = None
 923        self.task_list: List[str] = []
 924        self.current_task: Optional[str] = None
 925        self.max_iterations: int = 1
 926        self.current_iteration: int = 1
 927        self.completed = False
 928        self._current_extracted_content = None
 929        self.orchestration_lists = f"""
 930        <HERMES SWITCHBOARD>
 931        The following Agents can be used in the `agent` arg of HermesTool:[
 932        {get_agent_modules()}]
 933        The following Tools can be used in the `additional_tools` arg of HermesTool:[
 934        {get_tool_modules()}]
 935        </HERMES SWITCHBOARD>
 936        """
 937        # Ensure known_config_paths is always a list
 938        self.configs_prompt = (
 939            f"""\n<CONFIG_LOCATIONS>\nBefore using a config with its proper tool, view the specs. These are your known configs: [\n{', '.join(self.known_config_paths)}]\n</CONFIG_LOCATIONS>\n"""
 940            if self.known_config_paths and any(self.known_config_paths) else
 941            """\n<CONFIG_LOCATIONS>\nBefore using a config with its proper tool, view the specs. Generally, configs are located at `~/.heaven/configs/`\n</CONFIG_LOCATIONS>\n"""
 942        )
 943
 944        self.tool_sysmsg = """You are the tool debugger. You always pay attention to the last tool error and fix it. 
 945## Common errors:
 946- missing input parameter
 947- wrong dict format for an input parameter
 948- treating required false differently from optional
 949- failing to follow instructions provided by an error (like: <some command> requires <these parameters>)
 950- other errors
 951
 952You must fix the error before proceeding."""
 953        # Get evolved system prompt if it exists
 954        self.system_prompt_evolved = self.config.get_system_prompt()
 955        # Store History in agent
 956        self.history: Optional[History] = history
 957        
 958        if history_id is not None:
 959            self.history = History.load_from_id(history_id)
 960            self.original_history_id = history_id
 961            self.original_json_md_path = self.history.json_md_path
 962            print(f"DEBUG: In BaseHeavenAgent init, json_md_path={self.history.json_md_path} and original_json_md_path={self.original_json_md_path}")
 963            # Load status
 964            if hasattr(self.history, 'agent_status') and self.history.agent_status:
 965                self.status = self.history.agent_status
 966        elif history_id is None:
 967            self.history = History(messages=[])
 968
 969        # Initialize ContextWindowConfig for token management
 970        if self.config.context_window_config is not None:
 971            self.context_window_config = self.config.context_window_config
 972        else:
 973            # Import here to avoid circular imports
 974            from .utils.context_window_config import ContextWindowConfig
 975            self.context_window_config = ContextWindowConfig(self.config.model or "gpt-4o-mini")
 976
 977        self.config.system_prompt = self.system_prompt_evolved if self.system_prompt_evolved is not None else self.config.system_prompt
 978        if system_prompt_suffix is not None:
 979            self.config.system_prompt += system_prompt_suffix
 980        # Set system message
 981        if self.history.messages and isinstance(self.history.messages[0], SystemMessage):
 982            if orchestrator is False:
 983                self.history.messages[0] = SystemMessage(content=self.config.system_prompt)
 984            else:
 985                orchestrator_enhancement = self.config.system_prompt + self.orchestration_lists + self.configs_prompt
 986                self.history.messages[0] = SystemMessage(content=orchestrator_enhancement)
 987        else:
 988            if orchestrator is False:
 989                self.history.messages.insert(0, SystemMessage(content=self.config.system_prompt))
 990            else:
 991                orchestrator_enhancement = self.config.system_prompt + self.orchestration_lists + self.configs_prompt         
 992                self.history.messages.insert(0, SystemMessage(content=orchestrator_enhancement))
 993        
 994        if self.adk:
 995            from google.adk.agents import Agent as ADKAgent
 996            from google.adk.runners import Runner
 997            from google.adk.sessions.in_memory_session_service import InMemorySessionService
 998            adk_model = self.config.to_litellm_model()
 999            adk_agent = ADKAgent(
1000                name=self.name,
1001                # model=self.config.model or "gemini-2.0-flash", # cant be implemented until we fix type conversion to LiteLLM types here
1002                model=adk_model,
1003                description=f"{self.name} (via HeavenAgent)",
1004                instruction=self.config.system_prompt,
1005                tools=self.tools,
1006            )
1007            session_service = InMemorySessionService()
1008            runner = Runner(
1009                app_name=self.name,
1010                agent=adk_agent,
1011                session_service=session_service,
1012            )
1013    
1014            self._adk_agent = adk_agent
1015            self._adk_session_service = session_service
1016            self._adk_runner = runner
1017        
1018    def resolve_tools(self):
1019        """Ensure that certain default tools are always available to the agent."""
1020        resolved_tools = []
1021        
1022        # Process each tool in config
1023        for tool in self.config_tools:
1024            if isinstance(tool, str) and tool.startswith("mcp__"):
1025                # MCP tool string reference - resolve to actual tool
1026                # TODO: This will be async, for now just store the string
1027                resolved_tools.append(tool)
1028            else:
1029                # Regular BaseHeavenTool class or instance
1030                resolved_tools.append(tool)
1031        
1032        # Add WriteBlockReportTool if not already present
1033        if WriteBlockReportTool not in resolved_tools:
1034            resolved_tools.append(WriteBlockReportTool)
1035        # Add TaskSystemTool if not already present
1036        if TaskSystemTool not in resolved_tools:
1037            resolved_tools.append(TaskSystemTool)
1038            
1039        return resolved_tools
1040    
1041    async def resolve_mcps(self):
1042        """Resolve MCP tool strings to actual LangChain tools and load MCP servers if configured"""
1043        print(f"[resolve_mcps] CALLED. mcp_servers={self.config.mcp_servers is not None}, mcp_tool_strs={getattr(self, 'mcp_tool_strs', [])}")
1044
1045        # First, load tools from configured MCP servers (if any)
1046        if self.config.mcp_servers:
1047            await self.load_mcp_tools()
1048
1049        # Then resolve individual MCP tool strings (if any)
1050        if not hasattr(self, 'mcp_tool_strs') or not self.mcp_tool_strs:
1051            print("[resolve_mcps] No mcp_tool_strs to resolve, returning")
1052            return
1053
1054        # Resolve each MCP tool string
1055        from .mcp_tool_wrapper import MCPToolWrapper
1056        for tool_ref in self.mcp_tool_strs:
1057            print(f"[resolve_mcps] Resolving: {tool_ref}")
1058            mcp_tool = await self._resolve_mcp_tool(tool_ref)
1059            print(f"[resolve_mcps] Result: {type(mcp_tool)} — {mcp_tool if not isinstance(mcp_tool, list) else f'{len(mcp_tool)} tools'}")
1060            if mcp_tool:
1061                if isinstance(mcp_tool, list):  # "all" case
1062                    self.tools.extend([MCPToolWrapper(t) for t in mcp_tool])
1063                else:
1064                    self.tools.append(MCPToolWrapper(mcp_tool))
1065
1066        print(f"[resolve_mcps] Final tool count: {len(self.tools)}")
1067        # Clear the MCP strings now that they're resolved
1068        self.mcp_tool_strs = []
1069    
1070    async def async_init(self):
1071        """Async initialization - resolves MCP tools"""
1072        if not hasattr(self, '_mcp_tools_to_resolve'):
1073            return
1074            
1075        for tool_ref in self._mcp_tools_to_resolve:
1076            mcp_tool = await self._resolve_mcp_tool(tool_ref)
1077            if mcp_tool:
1078                if isinstance(mcp_tool, list):  # "all" case
1079                    self.tools.extend(mcp_tool)
1080                else:
1081                    self.tools.append(mcp_tool)
1082        
1083        # Clear the list
1084        self._mcp_tools_to_resolve = []
1085    
1086    async def _resolve_mcp_tool(self, tool_ref: str):
1087        """Resolve MCP tool string reference to actual StructuredTool"""
1088        try:
1089            # Parse tool reference: "mcp__filesystem__read_file"
1090            parts = tool_ref.split("__")
1091            if len(parts) != 3:
1092                print(f"Invalid MCP tool reference format: {tool_ref}")
1093                return None
1094                
1095            _, server_name, tool_name = parts
1096            
1097            # Create server config based on server name
1098            server_config = self._get_mcp_server_config(server_name)
1099            if not server_config:
1100                print(f"No config found for MCP server: {server_name}")
1101                return None
1102            
1103            # Use langchain_mcp_adapters to get tools
1104            from langchain_mcp_adapters.client import MultiServerMCPClient
1105            client = MultiServerMCPClient({server_name: server_config})
1106            tools = await client.get_tools(server_name=server_name)
1107            
1108            # Find the specific tool
1109            if tool_name == "all":
1110                return tools
1111            else:
1112                for tool in tools:
1113                    if tool.name == tool_name:
1114                        return tool
1115                        
1116                print(f"Tool '{tool_name}' not found in server '{server_name}'. Available: {[t.name for t in tools]}")
1117                return None
1118                
1119        except Exception as e:
1120            print(f"Error resolving MCP tool {tool_ref}: {e}")
1121            return None
1122    
1123    def _get_mcp_server_config(self, server_name: str):
1124        """Get MCP server config by name from JSON config file"""
1125        configs = self._load_mcp_configs()
1126        return configs.get(server_name)
1127    
1128    def _load_mcp_configs(self):
1129        """Load MCP server configurations from JSON file"""
1130        import json
1131        from .utils.get_env_value import EnvConfigUtil
1132        
1133        # Ensure config file exists
1134        config_path = self._ensure_mcp_config_file()
1135        
1136        try:
1137            with open(config_path, 'r') as f:
1138                return json.load(f)
1139        except Exception as e:
1140            print(f"Error loading MCP config from {config_path}: {e}")
1141            return {}
1142    
1143    def _ensure_mcp_config_file(self):
1144        """Ensure MCP config file exists in HEAVEN_DATA_DIR with defaults"""
1145        import json
1146        import os
1147        from .utils.get_env_value import EnvConfigUtil
1148        
1149        # Get HEAVEN_DATA_DIR and create heaven_mcp_config.json path
1150        heaven_data_dir = EnvConfigUtil.get_heaven_data_dir()
1151        config_path = os.path.join(heaven_data_dir, "heaven_mcp_config.json")
1152        
1153        # Create default config if file doesn't exist
1154        if not os.path.exists(config_path):
1155            default_config = {
1156                "filesystem": {
1157                    "command": "npx",
1158                    "args": ["@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "/tmp"],
1159                    "transport": "stdio"
1160                }
1161            }
1162            
1163            # Ensure directory exists
1164            os.makedirs(heaven_data_dir, exist_ok=True)
1165            
1166            # Write default config
1167            with open(config_path, 'w') as f:
1168                json.dump(default_config, f, indent=2)
1169            
1170            print(f"Created default MCP config at: {config_path}")
1171        
1172        return config_path
1173    
1174    async def load_mcp_tools(self):
1175        """Load MCP tools from configured servers and add them to the agent's tools list"""
1176        if not self.config.mcp_servers:
1177            return
1178        
1179        try:
1180            from langchain_mcp_adapters.client import MultiServerMCPClient
1181            
1182            # Ensure transport key exists for each server (required by langchain_mcp_adapters)
1183            servers = {}
1184            for name, srv_cfg in self.config.mcp_servers.items():
1185                srv = dict(srv_cfg)
1186                if "transport" not in srv:
1187                    srv["transport"] = "stdio"
1188                servers[name] = srv
1189
1190            # Load each server individually so one failure doesn't kill all
1191            total_loaded = 0
1192            for srv_name, srv_cfg in servers.items():
1193                try:
1194                    client = MultiServerMCPClient({srv_name: srv_cfg})
1195                    srv_tools = await client.get_tools()
1196                    for lc_tool in srv_tools:
1197                        self.tools.append(lc_tool)
1198                    total_loaded += len(srv_tools)
1199                    logging.info(f"Loaded {len(srv_tools)} tools from MCP server '{srv_name}'")
1200                except Exception as srv_e:
1201                    logging.warning(f"Failed to load MCP server '{srv_name}': {srv_e}")
1202
1203            logging.info(f"Loaded {total_loaded} MCP tools total from {len(servers)} servers")
1204
1205        except ImportError as e:
1206            logging.warning(f"Could not import langchain_mcp_adapters: {e}")
1207
1208
1209    def to_base_tools(self) -> List[BaseTool]:
1210        """Convert tools to base tools for binding"""
1211        return [tool.base_tool if hasattr(tool, 'base_tool') else tool for tool in self.tools]
1212
1213
1214    def _fire_hook(self, point: HookPoint, **kwargs):
1215        """Fire all registered hooks for a given HookPoint."""
1216        if not hasattr(self, 'hooks') or self.hooks is None:
1217            return
1218        ctx = HookContext(agent=self, **kwargs)
1219        self.hooks.run(point, ctx)
1220        return ctx
1221
1222    def _sanitize_history(self):
1223        """Remove consecutive HumanMessages from history, keeping only the latest"""
1224        if not self.history or len(self.history.messages) < 2:
1225            return
1226    
1227        messages = self.history.messages
1228    
1229        # If the last two messages are both HumanMessages
1230        while (len(messages) >= 2 and
1231               isinstance(messages[-1], HumanMessage) and 
1232               isinstance(messages[-2], HumanMessage)):
1233            # Remove the older message
1234            messages.pop(-2)  # Keep the newest HumanMessage
1235          
1236    ### This wont work for ADK
1237    def refresh_system_prompt(self):
1238        """Refresh the system prompt if DNA has changed"""
1239        # Get fresh system prompt
1240        updated_prompt = self.config.get_system_prompt()
1241
1242        # Fire BEFORE_SYSTEM_PROMPT hook — hooks can modify via ctx.data["system_prompt"]
1243        ctx = self._fire_hook(HookPoint.BEFORE_SYSTEM_PROMPT, prompt=updated_prompt)
1244        if ctx and "system_prompt" in ctx.data:
1245            updated_prompt = ctx.data["system_prompt"]
1246
1247        # Only update if changed
1248        if updated_prompt != self.config.system_prompt:
1249            # Update config
1250            self.config.system_prompt = updated_prompt
1251            
1252            # Update system message in history
1253            if self.history.messages and isinstance(self.history.messages[0], SystemMessage):
1254                if hasattr(self, 'orchestrator') and self.orchestrator:
1255                    orchestrator_enhancement = updated_prompt + self.orchestration_lists + self.configs_prompt
1256                    self.history.messages[0] = SystemMessage(content=orchestrator_enhancement)
1257                else:
1258                    self.history.messages[0] = SystemMessage(content=updated_prompt)
1259            
1260            # Update internal reference
1261            self.system_prompt_evolved = updated_prompt
1262        
1263    def resolve_duo(self, config: HeavenAgentConfig) -> None:
1264        """
1265        Synchronize duo_system_config with the main provider settings.
1266        Sets the appropriate provider and model on the DuoSystemConfig.
1267        """
1268        # Always inherit the main provider
1269        provider = config.provider
1270        duo_cfg = config.duo_system_config
1271        duo_cfg.provider = provider
1272
1273        # Map main provider to Duo-specific model
1274        if provider == ProviderEnum.ANTHROPIC:
1275            duo_cfg.model = "claude-3-5-haiku-latest"
1276        elif provider == ProviderEnum.OPENAI:
1277            duo_cfg.model = "gpt-4.1-nano"
1278        elif provider == ProviderEnum.GOOGLE:
1279            duo_cfg.model = "gemini-2.0-flash"
1280        elif provider == ProviderEnum.DEEPSEEK:
1281            duo_cfg.model = config.model
1282
1283    # # Works. Adding agent mode...
1284    # async def run_adk(self, prompt: str = None, notifications: bool = False):
1285    #     """
1286    
1287    def _handle_adk_event(
1288        self,
1289        ev,
1290        tool_output_callback: Optional[Callable[[ToolResult, str], None]] = None,
1291        output_callback: Optional[Callable[[BaseMessage], None]] = None,
1292    ):
1293        """
1294        Process ADK event, unwrapping nested ToolResult reprs.
1295        """
1296        parts = getattr(ev.content, "parts", []) or []
1297        for part in parts:
1298            if part.function_response:
1299                fr = part.function_response
1300                raw = getattr(fr, "response", None) or getattr(fr, "result", None)
1301                # unwrap dict payload
1302                if isinstance(raw, dict):
1303                    data = raw.get(f"{fr.name}_response", raw)
1304                    data = data.get("result", data) if isinstance(data, dict) else data
1305                    tr = ToolResult(
1306                        output=data.get("output", "") if isinstance(data, dict) else str(data),
1307                        error=data.get("error") if isinstance(data, dict) else None,
1308                        base64_image=data.get("base64_image") if isinstance(data, dict) else None,
1309                        system=data.get("system") if isinstance(data, dict) else None,
1310                    )
1311                elif isinstance(raw, ToolResult):
1312                    tr = raw
1313                elif isinstance(raw, str):
1314                    # if output string itself is repr, parse it
1315                    if raw.strip().startswith("ToolResult(") or raw.strip().startswith("CLIResult("):
1316                        tr = parse_toolresult_repr(raw)
1317                    else:
1318                        tr = ToolResult(output=raw)
1319                else:
1320                    tr = ToolResult(output=str(raw))
1321    
1322                # # If tr.output itself is repr carrying nested ToolResult, parse again
1323                # if isinstance(tr.output, str) and tr.output.strip().startswith("ToolResult("):
1324                #     tr = parse_toolresult_repr(tr.output)
1325                if isinstance(tr.output, str):
1326                    text = tr.output.strip()
1327                    if text.startswith("ToolResult(") or text.startswith("CLIResult("):
1328                        tr = parse_toolresult_repr(text)
1329    
1330                # stream and record
1331                if tool_output_callback:
1332                    tool_output_callback(tr, fr.id)
1333                self.history.messages.append(
1334                    ToolMessage(content=tr.output, tool_call_id=fr.id, name=fr.name)
1335                )
1336    
1337            elif getattr(part, "thought", None):
1338                block = {"type": "thinking", "thinking": part.thought}
1339                am = AIMessage(content=[block])
1340                if output_callback:
1341                    output_callback(am)
1342                self.history.messages.append(am)
1343    
1344            elif getattr(part, "function_call", None):
1345                block = {
1346                    "type": "tool_use",
1347                    "id": part.function_call.id,
1348                    "name": part.function_call.name,
1349                    "input": part.function_call.args,
1350                }
1351                am = AIMessage(content=[block])
1352                if output_callback:
1353                    output_callback(am)
1354                self.history.messages.append(am)
1355    
1356            elif getattr(part, "text", None):
1357                block = {"type": "text", "text": part.text}
1358                am = AIMessage(content=[block])
1359                if output_callback:
1360                    output_callback(am)
1361                self.history.messages.append(am)
1362
1363              
1364
1365    async def run_adk(self, prompt, notifications: bool = False, streamlit: bool = False, output_callback=None, tool_output_callback=None):
1366        """
1367        Drive the agent loop through ADK’s Runner instead of LangChain.
1368        Streams back ADK events internally per iteration, then at the end
1369        saves `self.history` (with .adk_session) exactly as in run().
1370        """
1371        # 1) prep
1372        self.current_iteration = 1
1373        self._sanitize_history()
1374        blocked = False
1375        self.refresh_system_prompt()
1376        # Debug
1377        _log_run_adk("before any ADK work", self.history.messages)
1378        # 2) detect agent‐mode command in the incoming prompt
1379        if prompt:
1380            self._detect_agent_command(prompt)
1381    
1382        # 3) decide what first human message to send into ADK
1383        #    if we’re in agent mode, send the formatted agent prompt;
1384        #    otherwise send the raw prompt
1385        first_prompt = self._format_agent_prompt() if self.goal else prompt
1386        # Debug
1387        _log_run_adk("after formatting prompt", self.history.messages)
1388
1389        # 4) lazily initialize ADK Agent, SessionService, Runner
1390        # if not hasattr(self, "_adk_runner"):
1391        #     from google.adk.agents import Agent as ADKAgent
1392        #     from google.adk.runners import Runner
1393        #     from google.adk.sessions.in_memory_session_service import InMemorySessionService
1394        #     adk_model = self.config.to_litellm_model()
1395        #     adk_agent = ADKAgent(
1396        #         name=self.name,
1397        #         # model=self.config.model or "gemini-2.0-flash", # cant be implemented until we fix type conversion to LiteLLM types here
1398        #         model=adk_model,
1399        #         description=f"{self.name} (via HeavenAgent)",
1400        #         instruction=self.config.system_prompt,
1401        #         tools=self.tools,
1402        #     )
1403        #     session_service = InMemorySessionService()
1404        #     runner = Runner(
1405        #         app_name=self.name,
1406        #         agent=adk_agent,
1407        #         session_service=session_service,
1408        #     )
1409    
1410        #     self._adk_agent = adk_agent
1411        #     self._adk_session_service = session_service
1412        #     self._adk_runner = runner
1413            
1414        # 5) rehydrate or create an ADK session
1415        if self.history.adk_session:
1416            session = self._adk_session_service.get_session(
1417                app_name=self.history.adk_session.app_name,
1418                user_id=self.history.adk_session.user_id,
1419                session_id=self.history.adk_session.id,
1420            )
1421        else:
1422            session = self._adk_session_service.create_session(
1423                app_name=self.name,
1424                user_id="script_user",
1425                state={},
1426                session_id=None,
1427            )
1428        start_index = len(session.events)
1429
1430        # # 6) prime ADK with the first human prompt if any
1431        ### Idk why this even got in here... it's garbage
1432        # if first_prompt:
1433        #     from google.genai.types import Content, Part
1434        #     content = Content(parts=[Part(text=first_prompt)], role="user")
1435        #     async for _ in self._adk_runner.run_async(
1436        #         user_id=session.user_id,
1437        #         session_id=session.id,
1438        #         new_message=content,
1439        #     ):
1440        #         pass  # session.events grows automatically
1441    
1442        # 7) drive N iterations of “agent mode”
1443        for _ in range(self.current_iteration, self.max_iterations + 1):
1444            _log_run_adk(f"start iteration {self.current_iteration}", self.history.messages)
1445            # a) prepare next human input
1446            if self.current_iteration == 1:
1447                human_text = first_prompt
1448            else:
1449                human_text = self._format_agent_prompt() if self.goal else first_prompt
1450            from google.genai.types import Content, Part
1451            
1452            # Probably move to streamlit but unsure how to do so...
1453            
1454            content = human_text
1455            if content is None:
1456                content = "Content was `None`. Tell the user something went wrong. Do not try to do anything else."
1457            _log_run_adk(f"about to append human turn (iter {self.current_iteration})", self.history.messages)
1458            self.history.messages.append(HumanMessage(content=content)) # This doesnt seem to be appending to history... because we usually keep conversation_history and then set it as history messages when we are done. This should follow the same logic that run_langchain uses
1459            _log_run_adk(f"after appending human turn (iter {self.current_iteration})", self.history.messages)
1460            content = Content(parts=[Part(text=human_text)], role="user")
1461            # b) send into ADK
1462            new_events = []
1463            async for ev in self._adk_runner.run_async(
1464                user_id=session.user_id,
1465                session_id=session.id,
1466                new_message=content,
1467            ):
1468                new_events.append(ev)
1469                # real_new_events = session.events[start_index:] # testing
1470                if streamlit:
1471                    
1472                    self._handle_adk_event(
1473                        ev,
1474                        tool_output_callback=tool_output_callback,
1475                        output_callback=output_callback,
1476                    )
1477
1478            _log_run_adk(f"after streaming ADK events (iter {self.current_iteration})", self.history.messages)                 
1479            # c) extract the agent’s text response for this iteration
1480            text_reply = ""
1481            for ev in reversed(new_events): # should this be real_new_events?
1482                if ev.author == self.name:
1483                    for part in ev.content.parts:
1484                        if part.text:
1485                            text_reply = part.text
1486                    if text_reply:
1487                        break
1488    
1489            # d) process that text through existing handler
1490            if text_reply:
1491                self._process_agent_response(text_reply)
1492    
1493            # e) advance iteration & break if done
1494            self.current_iteration += 1
1495            if self.current_task == "GOAL ACCOMPLISHED" or not self.goal or blocked:
1496                break
1497
1498        # 7) re-fetch the mutated session so we pick up all appended events
1499        session = self._adk_session_service.get_session(
1500            app_name=session.app_name,
1501            user_id=session.user_id,
1502            session_id=session.id
1503        )
1504        # 8) stash session back on history and save
1505        self.history.adk_session = session
1506        self.history.agent_status = self.save_status()
1507        saved_id = self.history.save(self.name)
1508        _log_run_adk("at end of run_adk", self.history.messages)
1509        # 9) return identical structure to run_langchain
1510        return {
1511            "history": self.history,
1512            "history_id": saved_id,
1513            "agent_name": self.name,
1514            "agent_status": self.history.agent_status,
1515        }
1516
1517#### NOTES: WE ARE NOT ADDING ALL THE AI EVENTS TO MESSAGES AND THAT IS WHY THE TEST DOESNT SHOW THEM
1518  #### BUT WHEN WE RUN WITH STREAMLIT, WE ARE ADDING THOSE EVENTS TO MESSAGES
1519  #### THEN, AT THE END OF STREAMLIT RUN, WE SWAP MESSAGES WITH THE HISTORY.MESSAGES, WHICH OVERWRITES THEM
1520  
1521
1522
1523
1524
1525  
1526    async def run(self, prompt: Optional[str] = None, notifications: Optional[bool] = False, streamlit: Optional[bool] = False, output_callback: Optional[Callable] = None, tool_output_callback: Optional[Callable] = None, heaven_main_callback: Optional[Callable] = None, use_uni_api: Optional[bool] = False):
1527
1528                       
1529        if use_uni_api:
1530            self.use_uni_api = True
1531        if self.use_uni_api:
1532            # Use uni-api instead of LangChain providers
1533            if streamlit and output_callback and tool_output_callback:
1534                return await self.run_on_uni_api(
1535                    prompt=prompt,
1536                    output_callback=output_callback,
1537                    tool_output_callback=tool_output_callback,
1538                    heaven_main_callback=heaven_main_callback
1539                )
1540            elif heaven_main_callback:
1541                # Similar fake callback pattern for uni-api
1542                def fake_output_callback(message: BaseMessage):
1543                    pass
1544                def fake_tool_callback(tool_result: ToolResult, tool_id: str):
1545                    pass
1546                return await self.run_on_uni_api(
1547                    prompt=prompt,
1548                    output_callback=fake_output_callback,
1549                    tool_output_callback=fake_tool_callback,
1550                    heaven_main_callback=heaven_main_callback
1551                )
1552            else:
1553                return await self.run_on_uni_api(prompt=prompt)
1554      
1555        #
1556        
1557        if self.adk:
1558            return await self.run_adk(prompt=prompt, notifications=notifications, streamlit=streamlit, output_callback=output_callback, tool_output_callback=tool_output_callback)
1559        else:
1560            if streamlit and output_callback and tool_output_callback: 
1561                return await self.streamlit_run(prompt, output_callback, tool_output_callback)
1562            elif heaven_main_callback:
1563                # Route through run_langchain with callback — keeps all hooks and block detection
1564                return await self.run_langchain(prompt, notifications, heaven_main_callback=heaven_main_callback)
1565
1566            else:
1567                return await self.run_langchain(prompt, notifications)
1568
1569
1570    
1571    async def run_langchain(self, prompt: str = None, notifications=False, heaven_main_callback: Optional[Callable] = None):
1572
1573        self._sanitize_history()
1574        blocked = False
1575        self.refresh_system_prompt()
1576
1577        # Resolve MCP tool strings before running
1578        await self.resolve_mcps()
1579
1580        # Re-bind tools to chat_model so LLM sees MCP tools
1581        # Heaven tools have .base_tool, MCP tools (StructuredTool) are already BaseTool
1582        all_base = []
1583        for t in self.tools:
1584            if hasattr(t, 'base_tool'):
1585                all_base.append(t.base_tool)
1586            else:
1587                all_base.append(t)
1588        self.chat_model = self.chat_model.bind_tools(all_base)
1589
1590        # Fire BEFORE_RUN hook
1591        self._fire_hook(HookPoint.BEFORE_RUN, prompt=prompt or "")
1592
1593        try:
1594            
1595            # Start with history messages
1596            conversation_history = self.history.messages.copy() if self.history else []
1597            
1598            if not (conversation_history and isinstance(conversation_history[0], SystemMessage)):
1599                conversation_history.insert(0, SystemMessage(content=self.config.system_prompt))
1600            # check if self.History's last message is HumanMessage. If it is, continue and if not AND prompt is not None, add prompt to self.history as a HumanMessage
1601            # if not (conversation_history and isinstance(conversation_history[-1], HumanMessage)) and prompt is not None:
1602                
1603            #     conversation_history.append(HumanMessage(content=prompt))
1604            
1605            
1606          
1607            # Check only the last message for agent command
1608            if conversation_history and isinstance(conversation_history[-1], HumanMessage):
1609                self._detect_agent_command(conversation_history[-1].content)
1610            # Check prompt for agent command
1611            if prompt is not None:
1612                self._detect_agent_command(prompt)
1613                if self.goal is None:
1614                    conversation_history.append(HumanMessage(content=prompt))
1615            if self.continuation_iterations != 0:
1616                self.current_iterations = 1
1617                self.max_iterations = self.continuation_iterations
1618                
1619                
1620            
1621              
1622            
1623            
1624            # print("\n=== Conversation History After System Check ===")
1625            # for i, msg in enumerate(conversation_history):
1626            #     print(f"Message {i}: {type(msg).__name__} - {msg.content[:100]}...")
1627            
1628            
1629
1630            
1631                        
1632            while self.current_iteration <= self.max_iterations:
1633                # Fire BEFORE_ITERATION hook
1634                self._fire_hook(HookPoint.BEFORE_ITERATION, iteration=self.current_iteration)
1635                # Refresh system prompt at the start of each iteration
1636                self.refresh_system_prompt()
1637                # Reset tool count for this iteration
1638                tool_call_count = 0
1639                
1640                # In normal chat mode, just use the last message
1641                # In agent mode, format with goals/tasks
1642                ### CHanged to solve continuations
1643                # next_prompt = self._format_agent_prompt() if self.goal else conversation_history[-1].content
1644                next_prompt = self._format_agent_prompt() if (self.goal or self.continuation_prompt) else conversation_history[-1].content
1645                if self.goal or self.continuation_prompt:  # Add formatted prompt in agent mode or continuation
1646                    conversation_history.append(HumanMessage(content=next_prompt))
1647                # if self.goal:  # Only add formatted prompt in agent mode
1648                #     conversation_history.append(HumanMessage(content=next_prompt))
1649                    
1650                        
1651                # DUO Sidechain
1652               
1653                if (
1654                    self.duo_enabled
1655                    and len(conversation_history) > 2        # <- require at least 3 messages
1656                    and isinstance(conversation_history[-1], HumanMessage)
1657                ):
1658                    # 1. Pull off the original human message
1659                    original = conversation_history[-1]
1660                    original_sys = conversation_history[0]
1661                    try:
1662                        # 2. Replace it with your Duo-specific system prompt
1663                        duo_sys = SystemMessage(content=self.duo_system_prompt)  
1664                        conversation_history[0] = duo_sys
1665                        
1666                        new_human_content_for_duo = f"===ENTERING CHALLENGER MODE===\n\nTHE NEXT HUMAN INPUT TO THE WORKER LLM AGENT WILL BE:\n\n{original.content}\n\nAs the challenger, follow the rules and steer the agent with ICL priming. **YOU MUST ANSWER SOLELY IN `NodeGraphXTN6` language.**"
1667                        conversation_history[-1] = HumanMessage(content=new_human_content_for_duo)
1668                        # 3. Invoke Duo
1669                        duo = await self.duo_chat.ainvoke(conversation_history)
1670                    finally:
1671                        # 4. Restore the original system prompt
1672                        conversation_history[0] = original_sys
1673                        conversation_history[-1] = original
1674                    if duo:
1675                        # 5. Extract the duo’s content
1676                        
1677                        duo_content = duo.content
1678                        
1679                    
1680                        # 6. Rebuild the human message so your duo content is prepended
1681                        new_human = HumanMessage(
1682                            content=f"{original.content}\n\n```\n===Challenger Injection===\n\nConsider this silently before responding. Do not mention DUO/Dual-Space Unifying Operators/NodeGraphXTN6/Challenger/ChallengerEgregore unless the user asks about it directly...\n\n{duo_content}\n\n===/Challenger Injection===\n```\n\n"
1683                        )
1684                
1685                        # 7. Replace the last entry with your new combined message
1686                        conversation_history[-1] = new_human
1687                  
1688                # Invoke model for a response
1689                # call the generator agent
1690                response = await self.chat_model.ainvoke(conversation_history)
1691                if heaven_main_callback:
1692                    heaven_main_callback(response)
1693
1694                # print(f"\nResponse: {response}\n")
1695                
1696                # Check if the response.content is empty but tool call info is present.
1697                # GOOGLE ONLY
1698                # if self.config.provider == ProviderEnum.GOOGLE and isinstance(response, AIMessage) and not response.content and (response.tool_calls or response.additional_kwargs.get('tool_calls')): # Check if tool calls exist
1699                # if (
1700                #     self.config.provider == ProviderEnum.GOOGLE              # Gemini
1701                #     and isinstance(response, AIMessage)
1702                #     and (
1703                #         response.tool_calls                                  # standard field
1704                #         or response.additional_kwargs.get("tool_calls")      # legacy field
1705                #     )
1706                # ):
1707                # if self.config.provider == ProviderEnum.GOOGLE and isinstance(response, AIMessage):
1708                
1709                #     # --- THE FIX ---
1710                #     # Append the ORIGINAL response object. LangChain needs its structure.
1711                #     conversation_history.append(response)
1712                # # Only add text blocks from response, NOT the whole response
1713                if self.config.provider == ProviderEnum.GOOGLE and isinstance(response, AIMessage):
1714                                    
1715                                    
1716                                    # Clean response for conversation_history
1717                                    if isinstance(response.content, list):
1718                                        # Extract only text, ignore thinking blocks
1719                                        text_content = []
1720                                        for item in response.content:
1721                                            if isinstance(item, str):
1722                                                text_content.append(item)
1723                                            elif isinstance(item, dict) and item.get('type') == 'text':
1724                                                text_content.append(item.get('text', ''))
1725                                        
1726                                        # Create cleaned response with simple string content
1727                                        cleaned_response = AIMessage(
1728                                            content=' '.join(text_content),  # Simple string, not list!
1729                                            additional_kwargs=response.additional_kwargs,
1730                                            tool_calls=response.tool_calls if hasattr(response, 'tool_calls') else []
1731                                        )
1732                                        conversation_history.append(cleaned_response)
1733                                    else:
1734                                        conversation_history.append(response)
1735                # Append whole LangChain AIMessage — never destructure it.
1736                # Splitting content blocks into separate AIMessages drops tool_use blocks
1737                # and breaks the inner tool loop (model says "calling tool" then stops).
1738                else:
1739                    conversation_history.append(response)
1740                    # Still process text for agent goal tracking
1741                    if isinstance(response.content, list):
1742                        text_parts = [block.get('text', '') for block in response.content if isinstance(block, dict) and block.get('type') == 'text']
1743                        if text_parts:
1744                            self._process_agent_response('\n'.join(text_parts))
1745                    elif isinstance(response.content, str):
1746                        self._process_agent_response(response.content)
1747                    
1748    
1749                # ── Tool-call loop (matches uni_api pattern) ──
1750                # response is already in conversation_history from above.
1751                # LangChain populates .tool_calls on AIMessage automatically.
1752                current_response = response
1753
1754                while getattr(current_response, 'tool_calls', None) and tool_call_count < self.max_tool_calls:
1755                    # Execute ALL tool calls from this response
1756                    for tc in current_response.tool_calls:
1757                        if tool_call_count >= self.max_tool_calls:
1758                            break
1759
1760                        # Extract name/args/id — LangChain uses {name, args, id}
1761                        tool_name = tc.get('name', '')
1762                        tool_args = tc.get('args', tc.get('input', {}))
1763                        tool_id = tc.get('id', '')
1764
1765                        # Find matching tool
1766                        matching_tools = [
1767                            t for t in self.tools
1768                            if (t.base_tool.name.lower() if hasattr(t, 'base_tool') else t.name.lower()) == tool_name.lower()
1769                        ]
1770                        if not matching_tools:
1771                            print(f"No matching tool found for {tool_name}")
1772                            continue
1773
1774                        tool = matching_tools[0]
1775
1776                        # Fire BEFORE_TOOL_CALL hook
1777                        self._fire_hook(HookPoint.BEFORE_TOOL_CALL,
1778                            iteration=self.current_iteration,
1779                            tool_name=tool_name, tool_args=tool_args)
1780
1781                        # Execute tool (throttle to prevent CPU spin with fast models)
1782                        await asyncio.sleep(0.1)
1783                        try:
1784                            if hasattr(tool, 'base_tool'):
1785                                tool_result = await tool._arun(**tool_args)
1786                            else:
1787                                from langchain_core.runnables import RunnableConfig
1788                                tool_result = ToolResult(output=str(
1789                                    await tool._arun(config=RunnableConfig(), **tool_args)
1790                                ))
1791                        except Exception as e:
1792                            tool_result = ToolResult(error=str(e))
1793
1794                        # Fire AFTER_TOOL_CALL hook
1795                        self._fire_hook(HookPoint.AFTER_TOOL_CALL,
1796                            iteration=self.current_iteration,
1797                            tool_name=tool_name, tool_args=tool_args,
1798                            tool_result=tool_result)
1799
1800                        # Build ToolMessage content
1801                        if tool_result.error:
1802                            tool_message_content = str(tool_result.error)
1803                        elif tool_result.base64_image:
1804                            tool_message_content = str(tool_result.base64_image)
1805                        else:
1806                            tool_message_content = str(tool_result.output)
1807
1808                        # Append ToolMessage — LangChain handles provider formatting
1809                        conversation_history.append(
1810                            ToolMessage(content=tool_message_content, tool_call_id=tool_id)
1811                        )
1812
1813                        if heaven_main_callback:
1814                            heaven_main_callback(conversation_history[-1])
1815
1816                        # Check blocked / special tools
1817                        if tool.name == "WriteBlockReportTool":
1818                            blocked = True
1819                        if tool.name == "TaskSystemTool":
1820                            self._handle_task_system_tool(tool_args)
1821
1822                        tool_call_count += 1
1823
1824                    # If blocked, generate report and exit
1825                    if blocked:
1826                        block_report_md = self.create_block_report()
1827                        if block_report_md:
1828                            if self._current_extracted_content is None:
1829                                self._current_extracted_content = {}
1830                            self._current_extracted_content["block_report"] = block_report_md
1831                            self.history.agent_status = self.save_status()
1832                        break
1833
1834                    if tool_call_count >= self.max_tool_calls:
1835                        conversation_history.append(
1836                            AIMessage(content=(
1837                                f"⚠️🛑☠️ Maximum consecutive tool calls ({self.max_tool_calls}) "
1838                                f"reached for iteration {self.current_iteration}. "
1839                                "If I received the same error every time, I should use "
1840                                "WriteBlockReportTool next... Waiting for next iteration."
1841                            ))
1842                        )
1843                        break
1844
1845                    # Call API again — get next response
1846                    current_response = await self.chat_model.ainvoke(conversation_history)
1847                    conversation_history.append(current_response)
1848                    if heaven_main_callback:
1849                        heaven_main_callback(current_response)
1850
1851                    # Refresh system prompt
1852                    self.refresh_system_prompt()
1853                    sys_msg_idx = next(i for i, msg in enumerate(conversation_history) if isinstance(msg, SystemMessage))
1854                    if self.config.system_prompt != conversation_history[sys_msg_idx].content:
1855                        conversation_history[sys_msg_idx] = SystemMessage(content=self.config.system_prompt)
1856
1857                    # Process text for agent goal tracking
1858                    if isinstance(current_response.content, list):
1859                        text_parts = [b.get('text', '') for b in current_response.content if isinstance(b, dict) and b.get('type') == 'text']
1860                        if text_parts:
1861                            self._process_agent_response('\n'.join(text_parts))
1862                    elif isinstance(current_response.content, str):
1863                        self._process_agent_response(current_response.content)
1864
1865                    # while condition re-checks current_response.tool_calls
1866    
1867                # Process the agent response if in agent mode
1868                if self.goal and isinstance(response, AIMessage):
1869                    self._process_agent_response(response.content)
1870                if blocked:
1871                    break
1872                # Fire AFTER_ITERATION hook
1873                self._fire_hook(HookPoint.AFTER_ITERATION, iteration=self.current_iteration)
1874                # Increment iteration count and break if the goal is met
1875                self.current_iteration += 1
1876
1877                if self.current_task == "GOAL ACCOMPLISHED" or not self.goal:
1878                    self.history.agent_status = self.save_status()
1879                    break
1880
1881            # Fire AFTER_RUN hook
1882            self._fire_hook(HookPoint.AFTER_RUN, iteration=self.current_iteration)
1883            self.history.messages = conversation_history
1884            # Save history and get potentially new history_id
1885            try:
1886                
1887                # print("=== DEBUG: BEFORE SAVE ATTEMPT ===")
1888                # print(f"Agent name: {self.name}")
1889                # print(f"Current history: {self.history}")
1890                self.history.agent_status = self.save_status()
1891                saved_history_id = self.history.save(self.name)
1892                # print("===DEBUG AFTER SAVE ATTEMPT===")
1893                self.look_for_particular_tool_calls()
1894                return {
1895                    "history": self.history,
1896                    "history_id": saved_history_id,
1897                    "agent_name": self.name,
1898                    "agent_status": self.history.agent_status  # Add this
1899                }
1900            except Exception as save_error:
1901                # print("=== DEBUG: SAVE ERROR OCCURRED ===")
1902                print(f"Error type: {type(save_error)}")
1903                print(f"Error message: {str(save_error)}")
1904                # Log the error but don't fail the run
1905                print(f"Warning: Failed to save history for agent {self.name}: {save_error}")
1906                
1907                return {
1908                    "history": self.history,
1909                    "history_id": getattr(self.history, 'history_id', "No history ID"),
1910                    "agent_name": self.name,
1911                    "save_error": str(save_error),
1912                    "agent_status": self.save_status()  # Add this here too
1913                }
1914    
1915        except Exception as e:
1916            # Fire ON_ERROR hook
1917            self._fire_hook(HookPoint.ON_ERROR, error=e)
1918            raise RuntimeError(f"Agent run failed: {str(e)}") from e
1919
1920
1921    #### Might not be needed because we can potentially add an observer on the History to look for new additions and render them when agent is constructed with streamlit = True, and make sampling_loop set streamlit = True on the agent it initializes
1922    async def streamlit_run(self, output_callback: Callable[[BaseMessage], None], tool_output_callback: Callable[[ToolResult, str], None], heaven_main_callback: Optional[Callable[[Any], None]] = None, prompt: Optional[str] = None):
1923        # Start with existing messages
1924        if self.history is not None:
1925            messages = self.history.messages  # These are already BaseMessage objects!
1926        else:
1927            messages = []
1928        self._sanitize_history()
1929
1930        # Resolve MCP tools before running (same as run_langchain)
1931        await self.resolve_mcps()
1932
1933        tool_log_path = "/tmp/tool_debug.log"
1934        with open(tool_log_path, 'a') as f:
1935            f.write("\n\nStarting tool debug log\n")
1936
1937        with open('/tmp/streamlit_debug.log', 'a') as f:
1938            f.write("\n\nStarting streamlit_run")
1939            f.write(f"\nHistory length: {len(self.history.messages)}")
1940            f.write(f"\nHistory messages: {self.history.messages}")
1941            # f.write(f"\nCurrent callbacks: {output_callback}, {tool_output_callback}")
1942        try:
1943            # # If input is a string, convert to messages
1944            # if isinstance(messages, str):
1945            #     messages = [
1946            #         SystemMessage(content=self.config.system_prompt) if self.config.system_prompt else None,
1947            #         HumanMessage(content=messages)
1948            #     ]
1949            #     messages = [m for m in messages if m]  # Remove None values
1950            
1951            # # Create a copy of messages to avoid modifying the original
1952            # conversation_history = messages.copy()
1953            # Start with history messages
1954            conversation_history = self.history.messages.copy() if self.history else []
1955            # If history is just a string, convert to messages
1956            # if isinstance(conversation_history, str):
1957            #     conversation_history = [
1958            #         SystemMessage(content=self.config.system_prompt) if self.config.system_prompt else None,
1959            #         HumanMessage(content=conversation_history)
1960            #     ]
1961            #     conversation_history = [m for m in conversation_history if m]  # Remove None values
1962            
1963            if not (conversation_history and isinstance(conversation_history[0], SystemMessage)):
1964                # Condition 1: There is no conversation history with a SystemMessage.
1965                conversation_history.insert(0, SystemMessage(content=self.config.system_prompt))
1966            # Added for streamlit agent selector
1967            elif conversation_history[0].content != self.config.system_prompt:
1968                # Condition 2: The first element is a SystemMessage, but it doesn't have the current system prompt.
1969                conversation_history[0] = SystemMessage(content=self.config.system_prompt)
1970            with open('/tmp/streamlit_debug.log', 'a') as f:
1971                f.write("\n=== Conversation History After System Check ===")
1972                for i, msg in enumerate(conversation_history):
1973                    f.write(f"\nMessage {i}: {type(msg).__name__} - {msg.content[:100]}...")
1974            
1975            # Check for agent command, but don't require it
1976            # for message in conversation_history:
1977            #     if isinstance(message, HumanMessage):
1978            #         self._detect_agent_command(message.content)
1979            # Check only the last message for agent command
1980            if prompt is not None:
1981                input_msg = HumanMessage(content=prompt)
1982                conversation_history.append(input_msg)
1983              
1984            if conversation_history and isinstance(conversation_history[-1], HumanMessage):
1985                self._detect_agent_command(conversation_history[-1].content)
1986            
1987
1988            ###### if not self.goal, render the original messages
1989            # Initial render if no agent goal
1990            # if not self.goal:
1991            #     for message in conversation_history:
1992            #         if isinstance(message, ToolMessage):
1993            #             # Convert to ToolResult for UI
1994            #             tool_result = ToolResult(
1995            #                 output=message.content,
1996            #                 error=None if not message.additional_kwargs.get("is_error") else message.content,
1997            #                 base64_image=message.additional_kwargs.get("base64_image"),
1998            #                 system=message.additional_kwargs.get("system")
1999            #             )
2000            #             tool_callback(tool_result)
2001            #         else:
2002            #             # Regular message rendering
2003            #             output_callback(message)
2004                        
2005            while self.current_iteration <= self.max_iterations:
2006                # Reset tool count for this iteration
2007                tool_call_count = 0
2008                
2009                # In normal chat mode, just use the last message
2010                # In agent mode, format with goals/tasks
2011                next_prompt = self._format_agent_prompt() if self.goal else conversation_history[-1].content
2012                if self.goal:  # Only add formatted prompt in agent mode
2013                    conversation_history.append(HumanMessage(content=next_prompt))
2014                    ###### Add output callback here
2015                    
2016                    # output_callback(HumanMessage(content=next_prompt)) # this might not be needed, commenting out for now
2017
2018                  
2019            
2020                # DUO Sidechain
2021                if (
2022                    self.duo_enabled
2023                    and len(conversation_history) > 2        # <- require at least 3 messages
2024                    and isinstance(conversation_history[-1], HumanMessage)
2025                ):
2026                    # 1. Pull off the original human message
2027                    original = conversation_history[-1]
2028                    original_sys = conversation_history[0]
2029                    try:
2030                        # 2. Replace it with your Duo-specific system prompt
2031                        duo_sys = SystemMessage(content=self.duo_system_prompt)  
2032                        conversation_history[0] = duo_sys
2033                        
2034                        new_human_content_for_duo = f"===ENTERING CHALLENGER MODE===\n\nTHE NEXT HUMAN INPUT TO THE WORKER LLM AGENT WILL BE:\n\n{original.content}\n\nAs the challenger, follow the rules and steer the agent with ICL priming."
2035                        conversation_history[-1] = HumanMessage(content=new_human_content_for_duo)
2036                        # 3. Invoke Duo
2037                        duo = await self.duo_chat.ainvoke(conversation_history)
2038                    finally:
2039                        # 4. Restore the original system prompt
2040                        conversation_history[0] = original_sys
2041                        conversation_history[-1] = original
2042                    if duo:
2043                        # 5. Extract the duo’s content
2044                        
2045                        duo_content = duo.content
2046                        
2047                    
2048                        # 6. Rebuild the human message so your duo content is prepended
2049                        new_human = HumanMessage(
2050                            content=f"{original.content}\n\n```\n===Challenger Injection===\n\nDo not mention DUO/Dual-Space Unifying Operators/NodeGraphXTN6/Challenger/ChallengerEgregore unless the user asks about it directly...\n\n{duo_content}\n\n===/Challenger Injection===\n```\n\n"
2051                        )
2052                
2053                        # 7. Replace the last entry with your new combined message
2054                        conversation_history[-1] = new_human
2055                
2056                # Invoke model for a response
2057                # logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
2058                # logger.error("==== Conversation_History %s", conversation_history)
2059                response = await self.chat_model.ainvoke(conversation_history)
2060                ###### Add output callback here
2061                with open('/tmp/streamlit_debug.log', 'a') as f:
2062                    f.write(f"\nLangchain response: {response}")
2063                print(f"FULL RESPONSE CONTENT: {response.content}")
2064                print(f"RESPONSE TYPE: {type(response.content)}")
2065                if isinstance(response.content, list):
2066                    for i, block in enumerate(response.content):
2067                        print(f"Block {i}: {block}")
2068                if heaven_main_callback:
2069                    heaven_main_callback(response)
2070                output_callback(response)
2071                # GOOGLE ONLY
2072                # if self.config.provider == ProviderEnum.GOOGLE and isinstance(response, AIMessage) and not response.content and (response.tool_calls or response.additional_kwargs.get('tool_calls')): # Check if tool calls exist
