def use_mcp_via_server_manager( query: str, servers: Optional[List[str]] = None, api_url: str = 'http://host.docker.internal:9000') -> Dict[str, Any]: 
235def use_mcp_via_server_manager(
236    query: str,
237    servers: Optional[List[str]] = None,
238    api_url: str = DEFAULT_API_URL
239) -> Dict[str, Any]:
240    """Execute a query using the server manager (agentic approach)"""
241    return manager_query(query, servers, api_url=api_url)

Execute a query using the server manager (agentic approach)

def use_mcp_via_session_manager( server_name: str, tool_name: str, tool_args: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None, api_url: str = 'http://host.docker.internal:9000') -> Dict[str, Any]: 
243def use_mcp_via_session_manager(
244    server_name: str,
245    tool_name: str,
246    tool_args: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
247    api_url: str = DEFAULT_API_URL
248) -> Dict[str, Any]:
249    """
250    Execute a tool using the session manager (direct function approach).
251    
252    This function:
253    1. Starts a new session with the specified server
254    2. Executes the specified tool
255    3. Closes the session
256    
257    Args:
258        server_name: Name of the MCP server to use
259        tool_name: Name of the tool to execute
260        tool_args: Arguments for the tool
261        api_url: URL of the MCP-Use API service
262        
263    Returns:
264        Dict with tool execution result
265    """
266    try:
267        # Start session
268        start_result = start_session(server_name, api_url=api_url)
269        if start_result.get("status") != "ok":
270            return {
271                "status": "error",
272                "error": f"Failed to start session: {start_result.get('error', 'Unknown error')}"
273            }
274        
275        # Execute tool
276        execute_result = execute_session_tool(tool_name, tool_args, api_url=api_url)
277        
278        # Always close session, even if tool execution fails
279        close_session(api_url=api_url)
280        
281        # Return execute result
282        if execute_result.get("status") != "ok":
283            return {
284                "status": "error",
285                "error": f"Tool execution failed: {execute_result.get('error', 'Unknown error')}"
286            }
287        
288        return {
289            "status": "ok",
290            "server": server_name,
291            "tool": tool_name,
292            "args": tool_args,
293            "result": execute_result.get("result")
294        }
295    except Exception as e:
296        # Ensure session is closed on error
297        try:
298            close_session(api_url=api_url)
299        except:
300            pass
301        
302        return {
303            "status": "error",
304            "error": f"Error in session manager flow: {str(e)}"
305        }

Execute a tool using the session manager (direct function approach).

This function:

  1. Starts a new session with the specified server
  2. Executes the specified tool
  3. Closes the session

Args: server_name: Name of the MCP server to use tool_name: Name of the tool to execute tool_args: Arguments for the tool api_url: URL of the MCP-Use API service

Returns: Dict with tool execution result

def create_mcp_tool_info(server_name: str, tool_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]: 
173def create_mcp_tool_info(server_name: str, tool_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
174    """
175    Create tool information for an MCP tool.
176    
177    Args:
178        server_name: Name of the MCP server
179        tool_name: Name of the tool to wrap
180        
181    Returns:
182        Dict with information about the tool for HEAVEN tool creation
183    """
184    # Start a session with the server
185    start_result = start_session(server_name)
186    if start_result.get("status") != "ok":
187        return {
188            "status": "error",
189            "error": f"Failed to start session with {server_name}: {start_result.get('error', 'Unknown error')}"
190        }
191    
192    try:
193        # Get tool arguments schema
194        args_result = get_tool_args(tool_name)
195        if args_result.get("status") != "ok":
196            return {
197                "status": "error", 
198                "error": f"Failed to get args for {tool_name}: {args_result.get('error', 'Unknown error')}"
199            }
200        
201        # Get the tool description
202        tool_description = args_result.get("description", f"MCP tool {tool_name} from server {server_name}")
203        
204        # Convert MCP schema to Python args
205        python_args = convert_mcp_schema_to_python_args(args_result.get("args", {}))
206        
207        # Generate wrapper function
208        wrapper_function = generate_mcp_wrapper_function(server_name, tool_name, tool_description, python_args)
209        
210        # Create function name
211        func_name = f"mcp_{tool_name.replace('-', '_')}"
212        
213        return {
214            "status": "ok",
215            "server_name": server_name,
216            "mcp_tool_name": tool_name, 
217            "tool_description": tool_description,
218            "python_args": python_args,
219            "wrapper_function": wrapper_function,
220            "function_name": func_name
221        }
222    finally:
223        # Always close the session
224        close_session()

Create tool information for an MCP tool.

Args: server_name: Name of the MCP server tool_name: Name of the tool to wrap

Returns: Dict with information about the tool for HEAVEN tool creation

def create_heaven_tool_from_mcp_tool(server_name: str, tool_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]: 
226def create_heaven_tool_from_mcp_tool(server_name: str, tool_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
227    """
228    Create a HEAVEN tool from an MCP tool.
229    
230    Args:
231        server_name: Name of the MCP server
232        tool_name: Name of the tool to wrap
233        
234    Returns:
235        Dict with the created HEAVEN tool class
236    """
237    # Get tool information
238    tool_info = create_mcp_tool_info(server_name, tool_name)
239    if tool_info.get("status") != "ok":
240        return tool_info
241    
242    try:
243        from heaven_base import make_heaven_tool_from_docstring
244        
245        # Create a local namespace and execute the wrapper function
246        namespace = {}
247        exec(tool_info["wrapper_function"], namespace)
248        
249        # Get the function object
250        func = namespace[tool_info["function_name"]]
251        
252        # Create HEAVEN tool from the function
253        tool_class = make_heaven_tool_from_docstring(func)
254        
255        # Create a descriptive tool name
256        server_part = ''.join(word.capitalize() for word in server_name.replace('@', '').replace('/', ' ').replace('-', ' ').split())
257        tool_part = ''.join(word.capitalize() for word in tool_name.replace('-', '_').split('_'))
258        tool_name_clean = f"MCP{server_part}{tool_part}Tool"
259        
260        return {
261            "status": "ok",
262            "tool_class": tool_class,
263            "tool_name": tool_name_clean,
264            "server_name": server_name,
265            "mcp_tool_name": tool_name,
266            "description": tool_info["tool_description"]
267        }
268        
269    except Exception as e:
270        return {
271            "status": "error",
272            "error": f"Error creating HEAVEN tool: {str(e)}",
273            "tool_info": tool_info
274        }

Create a HEAVEN tool from an MCP tool.

Args: server_name: Name of the MCP server tool_name: Name of the tool to wrap

Returns: Dict with the created HEAVEN tool class

def create_all_tools_for_mcp_server(server_name: str, max_tools: Optional[int] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]: 
276def create_all_tools_for_mcp_server(server_name: str, max_tools: Optional[int] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
277    """
278    Create HEAVEN tools for all tools on an MCP server.
279    
280    Args:
281        server_name: Name of the MCP server
282        max_tools: Maximum number of tools to create (for testing)
283        
284    Returns:
285        Dict with information about created tools
286    """
287    # Start a session with the server
288    start_result = start_session(server_name)
289    if start_result.get("status") != "ok":
290        return {
291            "status": "error",
292            "error": f"Failed to start session with {server_name}: {start_result.get('error', 'Unknown error')}"
293        }
294    
295    try:
296        # Get all available tools on the server
297        tools_result = get_session_tools()
298        if tools_result.get("status") != "ok":
299            return {
300                "status": "error",
301                "error": f"Failed to get tools from {server_name}: {tools_result.get('error', 'Unknown error')}"
302            }
303        
304        available_tools = tools_result.get("tools", [])
305        
306        # Limit the number of tools if max_tools is specified
307        if max_tools is not None and max_tools > 0:
308            available_tools = available_tools[:max_tools]
309        
310        created_tools = []
311        failed_tools = []
312        
313        # For each tool, create a HEAVEN wrapper
314        for tool_name in available_tools:
315            print(f"Processing MCP tool: {tool_name}")
316            
317            # Close the session first (we'll reopen it in create_heaven_tool_from_mcp_tool)
318            close_session()
319            
320            # Create the HEAVEN tool
321            tool_result = create_heaven_tool_from_mcp_tool(server_name, tool_name)
322            
323            if tool_result.get("status") == "ok":
324                print(f"✓ Created HEAVEN tool: {tool_result['tool_name']}")
325                created_tools.append(tool_result)
326            else:
327                error = tool_result.get('error', 'Unknown error')
328                print(f"✗ Failed to create tool for {tool_name}: {error}")
329                failed_tools.append({"tool_name": tool_name, "error": error})
330        
331        return {
332            "status": "ok",
333            "server_name": server_name,
334            "tools_created": len(created_tools),
335            "tools": created_tools,
336            "failed_tools": failed_tools
337        }
338    finally:
339        # Always close the session
340        try:
341            close_session()
342        except:
343            pass

Create HEAVEN tools for all tools on an MCP server.

Args: server_name: Name of the MCP server max_tools: Maximum number of tools to create (for testing)

Returns: Dict with information about created tools

def get_mcp_server_tools(server_name: str) -> List[str]: 
349def get_mcp_server_tools(server_name: str) -> List[str]:
350    """
351    Get a list of all tools available on an MCP server.
352    
353    Args:
354        server_name: Name of the MCP server
355        
356    Returns:
357        List of tool names
358    """
359    # Start session
360    start_result = start_session(server_name)
361    if start_result.get("status") != "ok":
362        return []
363    
364    try:
365        # Get tools
366        tools_result = get_session_tools()
367        if tools_result.get("status") == "ok":
368            return tools_result.get("tools", [])
369        return []
370    finally:
371        close_session()

Get a list of all tools available on an MCP server.

Args: server_name: Name of the MCP server

Returns: List of tool names

def test_mcp_tool_conversion(server_name: str, tool_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]: 
373def test_mcp_tool_conversion(server_name: str, tool_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
374    """
375    Test the conversion of a single MCP tool to HEAVEN tool.
376    
377    Args:
378        server_name: Name of the MCP server
379        tool_name: Name of the tool to test
380        
381    Returns:
382        Dict with test results
383    """
384    print(f"Testing MCP tool conversion: {server_name} / {tool_name}")
385    
386    # Step 1: Get tool info
387    print("  1. Getting tool information...")
388    tool_info = create_mcp_tool_info(server_name, tool_name)
389    if tool_info.get("status") != "ok":
390        return {
391            "status": "error",
392            "step": "tool_info",
393            "error": tool_info.get("error")
394        }
395    print(f"     ✓ Tool description: {tool_info['tool_description'][:100]}...")
396    
397    # Step 2: Create HEAVEN tool
398    print("  2. Creating HEAVEN tool...")
399    heaven_tool = create_heaven_tool_from_mcp_tool(server_name, tool_name)
400    if heaven_tool.get("status") != "ok":
401        return {
402            "status": "error", 
403            "step": "heaven_tool",
404            "error": heaven_tool.get("error"),
405            "tool_info": tool_info
406        }
407    print(f"     ✓ Created tool: {heaven_tool['tool_name']}")
408    
409    return {
410        "status": "ok",
411        "tool_info": tool_info,
412        "heaven_tool": heaven_tool,
413        "message": f"Successfully converted {tool_name} to {heaven_tool['tool_name']}"
414    }

Test the conversion of a single MCP tool to HEAVEN tool.

Args: server_name: Name of the MCP server tool_name: Name of the tool to test

Returns: Dict with test results

def discover_and_create_agent_for_mcp_server( server_name: str, max_tools: Optional[int] = None, auto_register: bool = True) -> Dict[str, Any]: 
 29def discover_and_create_agent_for_mcp_server(
 30    server_name: str,
 31    max_tools: Optional[int] = None,
 32    auto_register: bool = True
 33) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 34    """
 35    Discover, register, and create a HEAVEN agent for an MCP server.
 36    
 37    This is the main workflow for taking an MCP server name and creating
 38    a complete HEAVEN agent that can use all tools from that server.
 39    
 40    Args:
 41        server_name: Name of the MCP server (e.g., "@wonderwhy-er/desktop-commander")
 42        max_tools: Maximum number of tools to include (for testing)
 43        auto_register: Whether to automatically register the server if not found
 44        
 45    Returns:
 46        Dict with agent creation results
 47    """
 48    print(f"=== Creating HEAVEN Agent for MCP Server: {server_name} ===\n")
 49    
 50    # Step 1: Check if server is already registered
 51    print("Step 1: Checking server registration...")
 52    registered_servers = list_servers()
 53    
 54    server_registered = False
 55    if registered_servers.get("status") == "ok":
 56        server_list = registered_servers.get("servers", [])
 57        server_registered = server_name in server_list
 58        print(f"  Server {'already registered' if server_registered else 'not registered'}")
 59    
 60    # Step 2: Register server if needed
 61    if not server_registered and auto_register:
 62        print(f"\nStep 2: Auto-registering server {server_name}...")
 63        register_result = discover_and_register(server_name)
 64        if register_result.get("status") != "ok":
 65            return {
 66                "status": "error",
 67                "step": "registration",
 68                "error": f"Failed to register server: {register_result.get('error', 'Unknown error')}",
 69                "server_name": server_name
 70            }
 71        print(f"  ✓ Server registered successfully")
 72    elif not server_registered:
 73        return {
 74            "status": "error",
 75            "step": "registration",
 76            "error": "Server not registered and auto_register=False",
 77            "server_name": server_name
 78        }
 79    
 80    # Step 3: Get available tools
 81    print(f"\nStep 3: Discovering tools on {server_name}...")
 82    available_tools = get_mcp_server_tools(server_name)
 83    if not available_tools:
 84        return {
 85            "status": "error",
 86            "step": "tool_discovery",
 87            "error": "No tools found on server",
 88            "server_name": server_name
 89        }
 90    
 91    if max_tools and max_tools > 0:
 92        available_tools = available_tools[:max_tools]
 93        print(f"  Limiting to {max_tools} tools: {', '.join(available_tools)}")
 94    else:
 95        print(f"  Found {len(available_tools)} tools: {', '.join(available_tools)}")
 96    
 97    # Step 4: Create HEAVEN tools
 98    print(f"\nStep 4: Creating HEAVEN tools...")
 99    tools_result = create_all_tools_for_mcp_server(server_name, max_tools)
100    if tools_result.get("status") != "ok":
101        return {
102            "status": "error",
103            "step": "tool_creation",
104            "error": tools_result.get("error"),
105            "server_name": server_name
106        }
107    
108    created_tools = tools_result.get("tools", [])
109    failed_tools = tools_result.get("failed_tools", [])
110    
111    print(f"  ✓ Created {len(created_tools)} tools")
112    if failed_tools:
113        print(f"  ⚠ Failed to create {len(failed_tools)} tools")
114    
115    # Step 5: Create agent configuration
116    print(f"\nStep 5: Creating agent configuration...")
117    agent_config = create_mcp_agent_config(server_name, created_tools)
118    
119    return {
120        "status": "ok",
121        "server_name": server_name,
122        "agent_config": agent_config,
123        "created_tools": len(created_tools),
124        "failed_tools": len(failed_tools),
125        "tool_details": created_tools,
126        "failed_tool_details": failed_tools,
127        "available_tools": available_tools
128    }

Discover, register, and create a HEAVEN agent for an MCP server.

This is the main workflow for taking an MCP server name and creating a complete HEAVEN agent that can use all tools from that server.

Args: server_name: Name of the MCP server (e.g., "@wonderwhy-er/desktop-commander") max_tools: Maximum number of tools to include (for testing) auto_register: Whether to automatically register the server if not found

Returns: Dict with agent creation results

def create_mcp_agent_config(server_name: str, tool_results: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> Dict[str, Any]: 
130def create_mcp_agent_config(server_name: str, tool_results: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
131    """
132    Create a HEAVEN agent configuration for an MCP server.
133    
134    Args:
135        server_name: Name of the MCP server
136        tool_results: List of tool creation results
137        
138    Returns:
139        Dict with agent configuration
140    """
141    # Extract tool classes from results
142    tool_classes = []
143    tool_names = []
144    
145    for tool_result in tool_results:
146        if tool_result.get("status") == "ok":
147            tool_classes.append(tool_result.get("tool_class"))
148            tool_names.append(tool_result.get("tool_name"))
149    
150    # Add session management tools
151    try:
152        from heaven_base.tools.mcp_session_tools import (
153            ConnectMCPSessionTool, DisconnectMCPSessionTool, GetMCPSessionStatusTool
154        )
155        if ConnectMCPSessionTool:
156            tool_classes.extend([ConnectMCPSessionTool, DisconnectMCPSessionTool, GetMCPSessionStatusTool])
157            tool_names.extend(["ConnectMCPSessionTool", "DisconnectMCPSessionTool", "GetMCPSessionStatusTool"])
158    except ImportError:
159        print("Warning: Session management tools not available")
160    
161    # Create clean server name for agent
162    server_part = ''.join(word.capitalize() for word in server_name.replace('@', '').replace('/', ' ').replace('-', ' ').split())
163    agent_name = f"MCP{server_part}Agent"
164    
165    # Create system prompt
166    system_prompt = f"""You are an AI agent with access to tools from the MCP server "{server_name}".
167
168MCP (Model Context Protocol) allows you to interact with external systems and services.
169
170IMPORTANT: Before using any MCP tools from {server_name}, you must:
1711. First use ConnectMCPSessionTool to connect to the server
1722. Then use the available MCP tools as needed
1733. Finally use DisconnectMCPSessionTool when done
174
175Your available tools include:
176{chr(10).join(f'- {name}: {name}' for name in tool_names)}
177
178Session Management:
179- ConnectMCPSessionTool: Connect to {server_name} before using its tools
180- DisconnectMCPSessionTool: Disconnect when finished
181- GetMCPSessionStatusTool: Check current session status
182
183Always follow proper session management to prevent errors."""
184    
185    return {
186        "agent_name": agent_name,
187        "system_prompt": system_prompt,
188        "tool_classes": tool_classes,
189        "tool_names": tool_names,
190        "server_name": server_name,
191        "tool_count": len(tool_classes)
192    }

Create a HEAVEN agent configuration for an MCP server.

Args: server_name: Name of the MCP server tool_results: List of tool creation results

Returns: Dict with agent configuration

def create_multi_server_mcp_agent( server_names: List[str], max_tools_per_server: Optional[int] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]: 
194def create_multi_server_mcp_agent(
195    server_names: List[str],
196    max_tools_per_server: Optional[int] = None
197) -> Dict[str, Any]:
198    """
199    Create a HEAVEN agent that can access multiple MCP servers.
200    
201    Args:
202        server_names: List of MCP server names
203        max_tools_per_server: Maximum tools per server (for testing)
204        
205    Returns:
206        Dict with multi-server agent configuration
207    """
208    print(f"=== Creating Multi-Server MCP Agent ===")
209    print(f"Servers: {', '.join(server_names)}\n")
210    
211    all_tool_classes = []
212    all_tool_names = []
213    server_details = {}
214    
215    # Process each server
216    for server_name in server_names:
217        print(f"Processing server: {server_name}")
218        
219        # Create agent for this server
220        server_result = discover_and_create_agent_for_mcp_server(
221            server_name, 
222            max_tools=max_tools_per_server,
223            auto_register=True
224        )
225        
226        if server_result.get("status") == "ok":
227            config = server_result["agent_config"]
228            all_tool_classes.extend(config["tool_classes"])
229            all_tool_names.extend(config["tool_names"])
230            
231            server_details[server_name] = {
232                "tool_count": config["tool_count"],
233                "created_tools": server_result["created_tools"],
234                "failed_tools": server_result["failed_tools"]
235            }
236            
237            print(f"  ✓ Added {config['tool_count']} tools from {server_name}")
238        else:
239            print(f"  ✗ Failed to process {server_name}: {server_result.get('error')}")
240            server_details[server_name] = {
241                "error": server_result.get("error"),
242                "tool_count": 0
243            }
244    
245    # Create combined system prompt
246    system_prompt = f"""You are an AI agent with access to tools from multiple MCP servers:
247{chr(10).join(f'- {name}' for name in server_names)}
248
249MCP (Model Context Protocol) allows you to interact with external systems and services.
250
251IMPORTANT SESSION MANAGEMENT:
252Before using tools from any MCP server, you must connect to that specific server:
2531. Use ConnectMCPSessionTool with the server name
2542. Use the MCP tools from that server
2553. Use DisconnectMCPSessionTool when finished with that server
256
257Available tools from all servers:
258{chr(10).join(f'- {name}' for name in all_tool_names)}
259
260Session Management Tools:
261- ConnectMCPSessionTool: Connect to a specific MCP server
262- DisconnectMCPSessionTool: Disconnect from current server
263- GetMCPSessionStatusTool: Check current session status
264
265You can switch between servers by disconnecting from one and connecting to another.
266Always follow proper session management to prevent errors."""
267    
268    return {
269        "status": "ok",
270        "agent_name": "MCPMultiServerAgent",
271        "system_prompt": system_prompt,
272        "tool_classes": all_tool_classes,
273        "tool_names": all_tool_names,
274        "server_names": server_names,
275        "total_tools": len(all_tool_classes),
276        "server_details": server_details
277    }

Create a HEAVEN agent that can access multiple MCP servers.

Args: server_names: List of MCP server names max_tools_per_server: Maximum tools per server (for testing)

Returns: Dict with multi-server agent configuration

def discover_popular_mcp_servers(limit: int = 10) -> List[str]: 
279def discover_popular_mcp_servers(limit: int = 10) -> List[str]:
280    """
281    Discover popular MCP servers from the Smithery registry.
282    
283    Args:
284        limit: Maximum number of servers to return
285        
286    Returns:
287        List of popular server names
288    """
289    print(f"Discovering popular MCP servers (limit: {limit})...")
290    
291    try:
292        # Use server manager to get server recommendations
293        query = f"What are the {limit} most popular and useful MCP servers available? List just the server names."
294        result = use_mcp_via_server_manager(query)
295        
296        if result.get("status") == "ok":
297            # Try to extract server names from the response
298            response_text = result.get("result", "")
299            
300            # Simple extraction - look for @server/name patterns
301            import re
302            server_pattern = r'@[\w-]+/[\w-]+'
303            servers = re.findall(server_pattern, response_text)
304            
305            if servers:
306                print(f"Found {len(servers)} servers: {', '.join(servers[:limit])}")
307                return servers[:limit]
308        
309        # Fallback to discovery API
310        print("Falling back to discovery API...")
311        discover_result = discover_servers(page_size=limit)
312        
313        if discover_result.get("status") == "ok":
314            servers = discover_result.get("servers", [])
315            print(f"Found {len(servers)} servers via discovery")
316            return servers[:limit]
317            
318    except Exception as e:
319        print(f"Error discovering servers: {e}")
320    
321    # Return known working servers as final fallback
322    fallback_servers = [
323        "@wonderwhy-er/desktop-commander",
324        "@modelcontextprotocol/filesystem",
325        "@modelcontextprotocol/postgres"
326    ]
327    print(f"Using fallback servers: {', '.join(fallback_servers[:limit])}")
328    return fallback_servers[:limit]

Discover popular MCP servers from the Smithery registry.

Args: limit: Maximum number of servers to return

Returns: List of popular server names

def create_smart_mcp_agent( task_description: str, max_servers: int = 3, max_tools_per_server: int = 5) -> Dict[str, Any]: 
330def create_smart_mcp_agent(
331    task_description: str,
332    max_servers: int = 3,
333    max_tools_per_server: int = 5
334) -> Dict[str, Any]:
335    """
336    Create an MCP agent automatically based on a task description.
337    
338    This function uses the server manager to recommend appropriate MCP servers
339    for the given task, then creates an agent with those capabilities.
340    
341    Args:
342        task_description: Description of what the agent should be able to do
343        max_servers: Maximum number of servers to include
344        max_tools_per_server: Maximum tools per server
345        
346    Returns:
347        Dict with smart agent configuration
348    """
349    print(f"=== Creating Smart MCP Agent ===")
350    print(f"Task: {task_description}\n")
351    
352    # Step 1: Use server manager to recommend servers
353    print("Step 1: Getting server recommendations...")
354    query = f"""Given this task: "{task_description}"
355
356What are the best MCP servers that would help accomplish this task? 
357Please recommend up to {max_servers} servers and explain why each would be useful.
358List the server names clearly."""
359    
360    recommendation_result = use_mcp_via_server_manager(query)
361    
362    recommended_servers = []
363    if recommendation_result.get("status") == "ok":
364        response = recommendation_result.get("result", "")
365        print(f"Server recommendations:\n{response}\n")
366        
367        # Extract server names from response
368        import re
369        server_pattern = r'@[\w-]+/[\w-]+'
370        recommended_servers = re.findall(server_pattern, response)[:max_servers]
371    
372    # Fallback if no servers found
373    if not recommended_servers:
374        print("No specific recommendations found, using popular servers...")
375        recommended_servers = discover_popular_mcp_servers(max_servers)
376    
377    print(f"Selected servers: {', '.join(recommended_servers)}")
378    
379    # Step 2: Create multi-server agent
380    print(f"\nStep 2: Creating agent with selected servers...")
381    agent_result = create_multi_server_mcp_agent(
382        recommended_servers,
383        max_tools_per_server=max_tools_per_server
384    )
385    
386    if agent_result.get("status") == "ok":
387        # Customize system prompt for the specific task
388        original_prompt = agent_result["system_prompt"]
389        task_specific_prompt = f"""TASK FOCUS: {task_description}
390
391{original_prompt}
392
393TASK-SPECIFIC GUIDANCE:
394You have been specifically configured to help with: {task_description}
395
396The recommended servers for this task are:
397{chr(10).join(f'- {server}' for server in recommended_servers)}
398
399Focus on using the tools that are most relevant to accomplishing the specified task."""
400        
401        agent_result["system_prompt"] = task_specific_prompt
402        agent_result["task_description"] = task_description
403        agent_result["agent_name"] = "MCPSmartAgent"
404    
405    return agent_result

Create an MCP agent automatically based on a task description.

This function uses the server manager to recommend appropriate MCP servers for the given task, then creates an agent with those capabilities.

Args: task_description: Description of what the agent should be able to do max_servers: Maximum number of servers to include max_tools_per_server: Maximum tools per server

Returns: Dict with smart agent configuration

def connect_mcp_session(server_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]: 
19def connect_mcp_session(server_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
20    """
21    Connect to an MCP server session.
22    
23    This function opens a session with the specified MCP server.
24    Agents should call this before using any MCP tools from that server.
25    
26    Args:
27        server_name (str): Name of the MCP server to connect to (e.g., "@wonderwhy-er/desktop-commander")
28        
29    Returns:
30        dict: Status and result information
31    """
32    try:
33        result = start_session(server_name)
34        if result.get("status") == "ok":
35            return {
36                "status": "success",
37                "message": f"Successfully connected to MCP server: {server_name}",
38                "server_name": server_name,
39                "session_info": result
40            }
41        else:
42            return {
43                "status": "error",
44                "message": f"Failed to connect to MCP server: {server_name}",
45                "error": result.get("error", "Unknown error"),
46                "server_name": server_name
47            }
48    except Exception as e:
49        return {
50            "status": "error",
51            "message": f"Error connecting to MCP server: {server_name}",
52            "error": str(e),
53            "server_name": server_name
54        }

Connect to an MCP server session.

This function opens a session with the specified MCP server. Agents should call this before using any MCP tools from that server.

Args: server_name (str): Name of the MCP server to connect to (e.g., "@wonderwhy-er/desktop-commander")

Returns: dict: Status and result information

def disconnect_mcp_session() -> Dict[str, Any]: 
56def disconnect_mcp_session() -> Dict[str, Any]:
57    """
58    Disconnect from the current MCP server session.
59    
60    This function closes the current MCP session.
61    Agents should call this after they're done using MCP tools.
62    
63    Returns:
64        dict: Status and result information
65    """
66    try:
67        result = close_session()
68        if result.get("status") == "ok":
69            return {
70                "status": "success", 
71                "message": "Successfully disconnected from MCP server",
72                "session_info": result
73            }
74        else:
75            return {
76                "status": "error",
77                "message": "Failed to disconnect from MCP server",
78                "error": result.get("error", "Unknown error")
79            }
80    except Exception as e:
81        return {
82            "status": "error",
83            "message": "Error disconnecting from MCP server",
84            "error": str(e)
85        }

Disconnect from the current MCP server session.

This function closes the current MCP session. Agents should call this after they're done using MCP tools.

Returns: dict: Status and result information

def get_mcp_session_status() -> Dict[str, Any]: 
 87def get_mcp_session_status() -> Dict[str, Any]:
 88    """
 89    Get the current MCP session status.
 90    
 91    This function checks if there's an active MCP session and provides
 92    information about available tools if connected.
 93    
 94    Returns:
 95        dict: Current session status and available tools
 96    """
 97    try:
 98        from heaven_base.utils.mcp_client import get_session_tools
 99        
100        tools_result = get_session_tools()
101        if tools_result.get("status") == "ok":
102            tools = tools_result.get("tools", [])
103            return {
104                "status": "success",
105                "message": f"Active MCP session with {len(tools)} tools available",
106                "session_active": True,
107                "available_tools": tools,
108                "tool_count": len(tools)
109            }
110        else:
111            return {
112                "status": "info",
113                "message": "No active MCP session",
114                "session_active": False,
115                "available_tools": [],
116                "tool_count": 0
117            }
118    except Exception as e:
119        return {
120            "status": "error",
121            "message": "Error checking MCP session status",
122            "error": str(e),
123            "session_active": False
124        }

Get the current MCP session status.

This function checks if there's an active MCP session and provides information about available tools if connected.

Returns: dict: Current session status and available tools

ConnectMCPSessionTool = <class 'abc.ConnectMcpSessionHeavenTool'>
DisconnectMCPSessionTool = <class 'abc.DisconnectMcpSessionHeavenTool'>
GetMCPSessionStatusTool = <class 'abc.GetMcpSessionStatusHeavenTool'>
class HeavenCLI: 
 23class HeavenCLI:
 24    """CLI client that talks to HEAVEN HTTP server."""
 25    
 26    def __init__(
 27        self,
 28        agent_config: Union[HeavenAgentConfig, str, type],
 29        server_url: str = "http://localhost:8080",
 30        session_id: Optional[str] = None
 31    ):
 32        # Handle different agent_config types
 33        if isinstance(agent_config, HeavenAgentConfig):
 34            self.agent_config = agent_config
 35            self.agent_name = agent_config.name
 36            self.tools = agent_config.tools
 37        elif isinstance(agent_config, str):
 38            # Path to config file - we'll need to implement loading
 39            raise NotImplementedError("Config file loading not yet implemented")
 40        elif isinstance(agent_config, type):
 41            # Agent class - extract name and tools
 42            self.agent_config = None
 43            self.agent_name = agent_config.__name__
 44            # Try to get tools from the agent class if it has them
 45            self.tools = getattr(agent_config, 'tools', [])
 46        else:
 47            raise ValueError("agent_config must be HeavenAgentConfig, path string, or agent class")
 48            
 49        self.server_url = server_url.rstrip('/')
 50        self.session_id = session_id
 51        self.history_id = None
 52        self.conversation_id = None
 53        self.conversation_title = None
 54        
 55    async def _ensure_session(self):
 56        """Ensure we have an active session."""
 57        if self.session_id:
 58            return
 59            
 60        # Start a new session
 61        async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
 62            try:
 63                async with session.post(f"{self.server_url}/api/session/start") as resp:
 64                    if resp.status == 200:
 65                        data = await resp.json()
 66                        self.session_id = data.get("session_id")
 67                        logger.info(f"Connected to server (session: {self.session_id})")
 68                        print(f"📡 Connected to server (session: {self.session_id[:8]}...)")
 69                    else:
 70                        logger.error(f"Failed to start session: {resp.status}")
 71                        raise Exception(f"Failed to start session: {resp.status}")
 72            except aiohttp.ClientConnectorError:
 73                raise Exception(f"Cannot connect to HEAVEN server at {self.server_url}. Is it running?")
 74    
 75    def _render_agent_message(self, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
 76        """Render agent message event."""
 77        content = data.get("content", "")
 78        return f"\n🤖 Assistant:\n   {content}\n"
 79    
 80    def _render_thinking(self, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
 81        """Render thinking event."""
 82        content = data.get("content", "")
 83        return f"\n💭 Thinking:\n   {content}\n"
 84    
 85    def _render_tool_use(self, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
 86        """Render tool use event."""
 87        tool_name = data.get("name", "unknown")
 88        tool_input = data.get("input", {})
 89        input_str = json.dumps(tool_input) if tool_input else "(no input)"
 90        return f"\n🔧 Tool Call: {tool_name}\n   Input: {input_str}\n"
 91    
 92    def _render_tool_result(self, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
 93        """Render tool result event."""
 94        output = data.get("output", "")
 95        # Truncate long outputs
 96        if len(output) > 500:
 97            output = output[:500] + "... (truncated)"
 98        return f"\n📋 Tool Result:\n   {output}\n"
 99    
100    def _render_event(self, event_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Optional[str]:
101        """Render a HEAVEN event for display."""
102        event_type = event_data.get("event_type", "UNKNOWN")
103        data = event_data.get("data", {})
104        
105        renderers = {
106            "USER_MESSAGE": lambda d: None,  # Don't echo user input
107            "AGENT_MESSAGE": self._render_agent_message,
108            "THINKING": self._render_thinking,
109            "TOOL_USE": self._render_tool_use,
110            "TOOL_RESULT": self._render_tool_result,
111            "SYSTEM_MESSAGE": lambda d: f"\n🔧 System: {d.get('content', '')}\n",
112            "CONVERSATION_COMPLETE": lambda d: None  # Don't render, just signals completion
113        }
114        
115        renderer = renderers.get(event_type)
116        return renderer(data) if renderer else None
117    
118    async def _send_message_and_stream(self, message: str) -> None:
119        """Send message and stream back events."""
120        await self._ensure_session()
121        
122        # Prepare request data
123        request_data = {
124            "text": message,
125            "agent": self.agent_name,
126            "tools": [str(tool) for tool in self.tools] if self.tools else [],
127            "history_id": self.history_id
128        }
129        
130        async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
131            # Send message
132            async with session.post(
133                f"{self.server_url}/api/session/{self.session_id}/message",
134                json=request_data
135            ) as resp:
136                if resp.status != 200:
137                    error_text = await resp.text()
138                    logger.error(f"Message failed ({resp.status}): {error_text}")
139                    raise Exception(f"Message failed ({resp.status}): {error_text}")
140            
141            # Stream events
142            logger.debug("Starting event stream")
143            print("⚡ Processing...", flush=True)
144            
145            async with session.get(
146                f"{self.server_url}/api/session/{self.session_id}/stream"
147            ) as stream_resp:
148                if stream_resp.status != 200:
149                    raise Exception(f"Stream failed: {stream_resp.status}")
150                
151                async for line in stream_resp.content:
152                    if not line:
153                        continue
154                        
155                    line_str = line.decode('utf-8').strip()
156                    if not line_str or not line_str.startswith('data: '):
157                        continue
158                    
159                    try:
160                        # Parse SSE data
161                        event_data = json.loads(line_str[6:])  # Remove 'data: ' prefix
162                        
163                        # Extract history_id if present
164                        if event_data.get("history_id"):
165                            self.history_id = event_data["history_id"]
166                            logger.debug(f"Updated history_id: {self.history_id}")
167                        
168                        # Check if this is a completion event
169                        if event_data.get("event_type") == "CONVERSATION_COMPLETE":
170                            logger.debug("Received CONVERSATION_COMPLETE, ending stream")
171                            break
172                        
173                        # Render the event
174                        rendered = self._render_event(event_data)
175                        if rendered:
176                            print(rendered, end="", flush=True)
177                            
178                    except json.JSONDecodeError:
179                        logger.debug("Skipped malformed SSE event")
180                        continue  # Skip malformed events
181                    except Exception as e:
182                        logger.error(f"Error processing event: {e}", exc_info=True)
183                        print(f"\n❌ Error processing event: {e}")
184    
185    def _show_main_menu(self):
186        """Display main menu for conversation options."""
187        print("\n" + "="*60)
188        print("🌟 " + " HEAVEN CLI Chat Interface ".center(56) + "🌟")
189        print("="*60)
190        print(f"\n🤖 Agent: {self.agent_name}")
191        if self.tools:
192            tool_names = [str(t).split('.')[-1] if hasattr(t, '__name__') else str(t) for t in self.tools]
193            print(f"🛠️  Tools: {', '.join(tool_names)}")
194        else:
195            print("🛠️  Tools: None configured")
196        print(f"📡 Server: {self.server_url}")
197        print("\n" + "="*60)
198        print("📋 CONVERSATION MENU")
199        print("="*60)
200        print("1️⃣  Start New Conversation")
201        print("2️⃣  Continue Existing Conversation")
202        print("3️⃣  List Recent Conversations")
203        print("4️⃣  Search Conversations")
204        print("5️⃣  Exit")
205        print("\n" + "="*60 + "\n")
206
207    def _show_chat_header(self):
208        """Display chat session header."""
209        print("\n" + "="*60)
210        if self.conversation_title:
211            print(f"💬 {self.conversation_title}")
212        else:
213            print("💬 Chat Session")
214        print(f"🤖 Agent: {self.agent_name}")
215        if self.conversation_id:
216            print(f"📝 Conversation ID: {self.conversation_id}")
217        print("="*60)
218        print("💡 Instructions:")
219        print("  • Type your message and press Enter")
220        print("  • Press Ctrl+C to return to main menu")
221        print("\n" + "─"*60 + "\n")
222
223    async def _handle_new_conversation(self):
224        """Handle starting a new conversation."""
225        print("\n🆕 Starting New Conversation")
226        print("─"*40)
227        
228        title = input("📝 Enter conversation title (or press Enter for auto-title): ").strip()
229        if not title:
230            title = f"Chat with {self.agent_name} - {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')}"
231        
232        tags_input = input("🏷️  Enter tags (comma-separated, optional): ").strip()
233        tags = [tag.strip() for tag in tags_input.split(",") if tag.strip()] if tags_input else []
234        
235        self.conversation_title = title
236        print(f"\n✅ Ready to start: {title}")
237        return tags
238
239    async def _handle_continue_conversation(self):
240        """Handle continuing an existing conversation."""
241        print("\n📖 Continue Existing Conversation")
242        print("─"*40)
243        
244        # List recent conversations
245        recent = list_chats(limit=10)
246        if not recent:
247            print("❌ No existing conversations found.")
248            return False
249        
250        print("\n📋 Recent Conversations:")
251        for i, conv in enumerate(recent, 1):
252            print(f"{i:2d}. {conv['title']}")
253            print(f"    💬 {conv['metadata']['total_exchanges']} messages | 📅 {conv['last_updated'][:19]}")
254        
255        try:
256            choice = input("\n🔢 Enter conversation number (or 'q' to go back): ").strip()
257            if choice.lower() == 'q':
258                return False
259            
260            conv_idx = int(choice) - 1
261            if 0 <= conv_idx < len(recent):
262                selected_conv = recent[conv_idx]
263                self.conversation_id = selected_conv['conversation_id']
264                self.conversation_title = selected_conv['title']
265                
266                # Load the latest history for context
267                latest_history_id = ConversationManager.get_conversation_latest_history(self.conversation_id)
268                if latest_history_id:
269                    self.history_id = latest_history_id
270                
271                print(f"\n✅ Continuing: {self.conversation_title}")
272                return True
273            else:
274                print("❌ Invalid selection.")
275                return False
276                
277        except ValueError:
278            print("❌ Invalid input.")
279            return False
280
281    async def _handle_list_conversations(self):
282        """Handle listing conversations."""
283        print("\n📋 Recent Conversations")
284        print("─"*40)
285        
286        conversations = list_chats(limit=15)
287        if not conversations:
288            print("❌ No conversations found.")
289            return
290        
291        for i, conv in enumerate(conversations, 1):
292            print(f"\n{i:2d}. 📝 {conv['title']}")
293            print(f"    🤖 Agent: {conv['metadata']['agent_name']}")
294            print(f"    💬 Messages: {conv['metadata']['total_exchanges']}")
295            print(f"    📅 Last updated: {conv['last_updated'][:19]}")
296            if conv['metadata'].get('tags'):
297                print(f"    🏷️  Tags: {', '.join(conv['metadata']['tags'])}")
298        
299        input("\n⏎ Press Enter to continue...")
300
301    async def _handle_search_conversations(self):
302        """Handle searching conversations."""
303        print("\n🔍 Search Conversations")
304        print("─"*40)
305        
306        query = input("🔎 Enter search query: ").strip()
307        if not query:
308            print("❌ No search query provided.")
309            return
310        
311        results = search_chats(query)
312        if not results:
313            print(f"❌ No conversations found matching '{query}'.")
314            return
315        
316        print(f"\n🎯 Found {len(results)} results for '{query}':")
317        for i, conv in enumerate(results, 1):
318            print(f"\n{i:2d}. 📝 {conv['title']}")
319            print(f"    💬 {conv['metadata']['total_exchanges']} messages | 📅 {conv['last_updated'][:19]}")
320            if conv['metadata'].get('tags'):
321                print(f"    🏷️  Tags: {', '.join(conv['metadata']['tags'])}")
322        
323        input("\n⏎ Press Enter to continue...")
324    
325    async def _chat_loop(self, tags: Optional[List[str]] = None):
326        """Run the main chat interaction loop."""
327        self._show_chat_header()
328        
329        print("\n" + "─"*60 + "\n")
330        print("💡 Type your message to start the conversation, or 'back' to return to menu")
331        print("─"*60)
332        
333        while True:
334            try:
335                # Get user input
336                user_input = input("💭 Your message: ").strip()
337                
338                if not user_input:
339                    continue
340                
341                # Check for exit commands
342                if user_input.lower() in ['back', 'exit', 'quit', 'menu']:
343                    print("\n🔙 Returning to main menu...\n")
344                    break
345                
346                # Send message and stream response
347                await self._send_message_and_stream(user_input)
348                
349                # If this was the first message and we got a history_id, create conversation record
350                if not self.conversation_id and self.history_id:
351                    conv_data = start_chat(
352                        title=self.conversation_title or f"Chat with {self.agent_name}",
353                        first_history_id=self.history_id,
354                        agent_name=self.agent_name,
355                        tags=tags or []
356                    )
357                    self.conversation_id = conv_data['conversation_id']
358                    print(f"\n📝 Conversation saved as: {self.conversation_id}")
359                
360                # Update conversation with new history if we have one
361                if self.conversation_id and self.history_id:
362                    try:
363                        continue_chat(self.conversation_id, self.history_id)
364                    except Exception as e:
365                        print(f"⚠️  Warning: Could not update conversation: {e}")
366                
367                print("\n" + "─"*50)
368                
369            except KeyboardInterrupt:
370                print("\n\n🔙 Returning to main menu...\n")
371                break
372            except Exception as e:
373                print(f"\n❌ Error: {e}")
374                print("Continuing...\n")
375    
376    async def run(self):
377        """Run the CLI with main menu system."""
378        print("\n🌟 Welcome to HEAVEN CLI Chat! 🌟")
379        
380        while True:
381            try:
382                self._show_main_menu()
383                choice = input("🔢 Enter your choice (1-5): ").strip()
384                
385                if choice == "1":
386                    # Start new conversation
387                    tags = await self._handle_new_conversation()
388                    # Reset conversation state
389                    self.conversation_id = None
390                    self.history_id = None
391                    await self._chat_loop(tags)
392                    
393                elif choice == "2":
394                    # Continue existing conversation
395                    if await self._handle_continue_conversation():
396                        await self._chat_loop()
397                    
398                elif choice == "3":
399                    # List conversations
400                    await self._handle_list_conversations()
401                    
402                elif choice == "4":
403                    # Search conversations
404                    await self._handle_search_conversations()
405                    
406                elif choice == "5" or choice.lower() == "quit" or choice.lower() == "exit":
407                    # Exit
408                    print("\n" + "="*60)
409                    print("👋 Thanks for using HEAVEN CLI! Goodbye!")
410                    print("="*60 + "\n")
411                    break
412                    
413                else:
414                    print("\n❌ Invalid choice. Please select 1-5.\n")
415                    
416            except KeyboardInterrupt:
417                print("\n\n" + "="*60)
418                print("👋 Thanks for using HEAVEN CLI! Goodbye!")
419                print("="*60 + "\n")
420                break
421            except Exception as e:
422                print(f"\n❌ Error: {e}")
423                print("Returning to main menu...\n")
424    
425    def run_sync(self):
426        """Run the CLI synchronously."""
427        try:
428            asyncio.run(self.run())
429        except KeyboardInterrupt:
430            pass

CLI client that talks to HEAVEN HTTP server.

HeavenCLI( agent_config: Union[HeavenAgentConfig, str, type], server_url: str = 'http://localhost:8080', session_id: Optional[str] = None) 
26    def __init__(
27        self,
28        agent_config: Union[HeavenAgentConfig, str, type],
29        server_url: str = "http://localhost:8080",
30        session_id: Optional[str] = None
31    ):
32        # Handle different agent_config types
33        if isinstance(agent_config, HeavenAgentConfig):
34            self.agent_config = agent_config
35            self.agent_name = agent_config.name
36            self.tools = agent_config.tools
37        elif isinstance(agent_config, str):
38            # Path to config file - we'll need to implement loading
39            raise NotImplementedError("Config file loading not yet implemented")
40        elif isinstance(agent_config, type):
41            # Agent class - extract name and tools
42            self.agent_config = None
43            self.agent_name = agent_config.__name__
44            # Try to get tools from the agent class if it has them
45            self.tools = getattr(agent_config, 'tools', [])
46        else:
47            raise ValueError("agent_config must be HeavenAgentConfig, path string, or agent class")
48            
49        self.server_url = server_url.rstrip('/')
50        self.session_id = session_id
51        self.history_id = None
52        self.conversation_id = None
53        self.conversation_title = None
server_url
session_id
history_id
conversation_id
conversation_title
async def run(self): 
376    async def run(self):
377        """Run the CLI with main menu system."""
378        print("\n🌟 Welcome to HEAVEN CLI Chat! 🌟")
379        
380        while True:
381            try:
382                self._show_main_menu()
383                choice = input("🔢 Enter your choice (1-5): ").strip()
384                
385                if choice == "1":
386                    # Start new conversation
387                    tags = await self._handle_new_conversation()
388                    # Reset conversation state
389                    self.conversation_id = None
390                    self.history_id = None
391                    await self._chat_loop(tags)
392                    
393                elif choice == "2":
394                    # Continue existing conversation
395                    if await self._handle_continue_conversation():
396                        await self._chat_loop()
397                    
398                elif choice == "3":
399                    # List conversations
400                    await self._handle_list_conversations()
401                    
402                elif choice == "4":
403                    # Search conversations
404                    await self._handle_search_conversations()
405                    
406                elif choice == "5" or choice.lower() == "quit" or choice.lower() == "exit":
407                    # Exit
408                    print("\n" + "="*60)
409                    print("👋 Thanks for using HEAVEN CLI! Goodbye!")
410                    print("="*60 + "\n")
411                    break
412                    
413                else:
414                    print("\n❌ Invalid choice. Please select 1-5.\n")
415                    
416            except KeyboardInterrupt:
417                print("\n\n" + "="*60)
418                print("👋 Thanks for using HEAVEN CLI! Goodbye!")
419                print("="*60 + "\n")
420                break
421            except Exception as e:
422                print(f"\n❌ Error: {e}")
423                print("Returning to main menu...\n")

Run the CLI with main menu system.

def run_sync(self): 
425    def run_sync(self):
426        """Run the CLI synchronously."""
427        try:
428            asyncio.run(self.run())
429        except KeyboardInterrupt:
430            pass

Run the CLI synchronously.

def make_cli( agent_config: Union[HeavenAgentConfig, str, type], server_url: str = 'http://localhost:8080') -> HeavenCLI: 
433def make_cli(
434    agent_config: Union[HeavenAgentConfig, str, type],
435    server_url: str = "http://localhost:8080"
436) -> HeavenCLI:
437    """Create a configured CLI instance.
438    
439    Args:
440        agent_config: HeavenAgentConfig instance, path to config file, or agent class
441        server_url: URL of HEAVEN HTTP server
442    
443    Returns:
444        Configured HeavenCLI instance
445    """
446    return HeavenCLI(
447        agent_config=agent_config,
448        server_url=server_url
449    )

Create a configured CLI instance.

Args: agent_config: HeavenAgentConfig instance, path to config file, or agent class server_url: URL of HEAVEN HTTP server

Returns: Configured HeavenCLI instance
